pages.content:
Meeting recording (https://corelogic-my.sharepoint.com/personal/jofranklin_cotality_com/_layouts/15/stream.aspx?id=%2Fpersonal%2Fjofranklin%5Fcotality%5Fcom%2FDocuments%2FRecordings%2FCMDB%20Launch%2013%2D20260115%5F230007%2DMeeting%20Recording%2Emp4&referrer=StreamWebApp%2EWeb&referrerScenario=AddressBarCopied%2Eview%2Eb2bc5166%2D3ced%2D4490%2Db570%2D129d97fc2ea5)
chat details
Landing page
Easily Build Custom Reports and Dashboards - Looker Studio
Looker Studio turns your data into informative reports and dashboards that are easy to read, easy to share, and fully custom.
can you please add group info in chat?
how would I go about claiming an asset as the responsible ISM where there is not currently an ISM assigned## 1. DISCUSSION TOPICS### Session Context and Logistics
Multi-Session Delivery Approach
• Second of three identical sessions launching CMDB first release• Content exactly the same across all three sessions• Attendees from previous session welcome to stay but not necessary• Attendees of this session do not need to join tomorrow's session• Different audience groups invited to each session for scheduling convenience### CMDB System Overview
What is CMDB (Configuration Management Database)
• Configuration management database built on Totality platform standard tooling• Technology stack: Airflow, Smart Data Platform, BigQuery• Ingests and normalizes data from dozens of different sources• Presentation layer currently using Looker Studio (subject to change)• Vision: House all IT assets with ownership information and relationships• Common repository for building use cases for different personasFirst Release Scope - Asset Coverage
• GCP Coverage: Approximately 95% of M com assets• AWS Coverage: Approximately 85% of M com assets (slightly less than GCP)• Key Differentiator: Ingesting AWS non-M com asset information for compute resources• Specific accounts/areas where non-M com data ingested (shown on screen during presentation)• Active Directory service accounts included• End User devices information included• Purchase software assets information includedEnterprise Meta Model - First Time Formalized
• Relationships between cloud metadata and applications established• Connection to org space and projects• Application information (used in change tickets)• Product line, BU, and Segment information• ELT information from Plan View• All relationships built out and accessiblePrimary Use Cases in First Release
• Repave compliance (two key compliance use cases)• Service account key compliance• Infrastructure tracking• Software and licenses• Laptops and mobiles (End User devices)• Cloud, app, and business metadata with relationships• Coverage reporting• Data quality reporting### Access and Navigation
Access Control Model
• Access through Active Directory group membership exclusively• Embedded in ILM (Identity Lifecycle Management) processes• Critical Change: Uses Totality identity, NOT Solutions identity• Different from pre-existing Looker Studio dashboards requiring Solutions identity• "Request access" button in Looker Studio should NOT be used• Must request via Savient → Cloud Group Access → Request AD group membership• 95% of invitees pre-added to Active Directory group• All access governed through group membership (no direct access)Dashboard Organization - Top Level Structure
• Logically grouped by use case• Infrastructure: Compliance use cases (repave and service account keys)• Purchase Software: Software GL information + renewal tracker• Mobile and Laptops: End User device metadata• Metadata: Cloud (project/account view), org space view, application view, business view, plus relationships• Analytics and Quality: Coverage reporting and data quality reportsNavigation Structure
• Drill downs go down to three levels• Arrow indicators show available drill downs• Fairly intuitive navigationStandard Filter Categories
• Repeatable Filters (Blue): Appear across different dashboards
• Organ space (from cloud metadata)• Operating cost owner• Workspace Fleda• Product line (from Plan View)• ELT (from Plan View)• Asset-Specific Filters: May repeat but less likely across all dashboards• Pertain to specific table or charting on given pageMCO (Minimum Container Object) Definition
• Cloud-agnostic term for project or account• GCP: Project• AWS: Account• Future: Supports three-cloud environment (currently two-cloud)• MCO Name dropdown shows list of projects or accountsTable Functionality
• All tables exportable via three-dot menu (top right)• Click "export" to download• May not have exact view needed; basic trending built in• Users can export and create own reporting/graphs/charting in ExcelLooker Studio Gotcha - Horizontal Scroll
• Critical: All tables have horizontal scroll• Many people assume information missing• Must check for scroll before assuming columns missing• Frequently asked question necessitating highlight in presentationDate Ranges and Data Freshness
• Default View: Last completed day (not current day)• Reason: Ensures data present in dashboard (avoids empty tables and null values)• Example: Video filmed on 12th defaults to 11th• Fresher Data Available: Depending on access time (especially US-based users)• Users can manually update date range to current day• Example shown: Updating from 11th to 12th shows instance count changeUpdate Frequency and Propagation
• Pipeline Run Frequency: Daily (exception: software assets different cadence)• Change Propagation Time: Up to 24 hours (can take up to 48 hours total)• Depends on exact time of day change made• Example: Destroy and rebuild VM may not show within 24 hours• Guidance: If issue persists beyond 72 hours, reach out to CMDB team• Most reported issues resolve with additional 24-hour waitMultiple Date Ranges and Load Timestamp
• Users can select multiple date ranges• All tables include load timestamp• Load timestamp enables trending analysis in Excel• Upcoming video demonstrates load timestamp in tablesChatbot Integration
• All standard operating procedures onboarded to Confluence Chatbot• Also available via Drivebot• Frequent questions can be addressed via chatbot• First port of call for common questions• Other support channels shared at session end### Repave Compliance Use Case
Dashboard Deprecation and Transition
• Existing Looker Studio dashboard now considered deprecated• Banner going up Monday (following presentation) with:
• Deprecation communication• Link to CMDB equivalent• Instructions for access• Old dashboard kept up for 4-5 weeks• Target removal date: Around February 20• Assumption: No issues raised during transition• Strong Advice: Switch to new dashboard immediatelyReason for Immediate Switch
• Compliance logic changed to better reflect security posture• Logic changes not reflected in old dashboard• New dashboard provides more accurate security assessmentKey Differences from Previous System
• Update Frequency:
• Previous: Weekly updates• New: Daily updates (frequently requested enhancement)• Data Consolidation:
• Single table combining:
• AWS M com• AWS non-M com (accounts from prior slide)• GCP instance data• Previously: Switching between views required OR no automated feed for some accounts• Exceptions Process Improvement:
• Previous: Ad hoc patch-in-place exception process• New: Formalized process
• Runs through Jira• ISM approval required• Updates BigQuery table• Table visualized in CMDB• Complete transparency into approved exceptions• Information no longer altercated• Compliance Logic Overhaul (Most Important Change):
• Previous Logic: Instance age only• Problem: Brand new instance built with old source image showed compliant• New Logic: Combines instance age compliance + image compliance• Determines final compliance status from both factorsCompliance Status Determination Logic
Combined Compliance Table
• Shows how overall compliance derived from:
• Instance age• Source image age• Unknown Category: Introduced for source images where age difficult to determine• Various reasons make age determination challenging• Team working to burn through unknown list (reduce number over time)• Important: Even if source image age unknown, non-compliant instance age results in non-compliant overall statusNavigation and Filtering
M com / Non-M com Filter
• Easy yes/no dropdown to filter out non-M com feed• No need to filter by project or account name patterns• Simplifies view for users wanting one or the otherRepave-Specific Filters
• Instance Age Rule:
• Consistent with prior reporting (no change)• Values: Compliant or Non-Compliant (no unknowns)• Image Age Rule (NEW):
• Based on three possible sources: a. Source image metadata (if available) b. Pattern match from Image Factory c. Inferred from image age using timestamp• Logic:
• Windows: 2 cycles of image age• Linux/Other: 2 cycles of image age (90 or 180 days)• Compliance Reason:
• Brief text description of why image age/source image based compliance has specific value• Transparency into determination method:
• Metadata-based• Inferred• Pattern match• Helps understand logic without guessing• Patching Information (NEW):
• Image Factory data (GCP only)• Combination of Qualys data + source image data• Determines missed patch cycles per VM (0, 1, or more)• Currently scanning ~400 VMs• Data now ingested into CMDB• PIP Exception Tracking:
• Note: Arrow in presentation pointed to wrong item (should point to PIP exception, not PIP compliance)• Tracks approved patch-in-place exceptions• Result of formalized Jira processPatch-in-Place Exception Handling
• Initial data: Same dataset feeding legacy dashboard• Valid for approximately 40 days• Review process with ISM during 40-day period• Actions: Reinstate exceptions, add new ones, allow some to expire• Current state: Exactly same information as previous Looker Studio dashboardFinal Compliance Status
• Located in specific column• Values: Compliant, Non-Compliant, UnknownDashboard Components - Detailed Walkthrough
Navigation Location
• Infrastructure section → Infrastructure Compute sectionDashboard Structure (Top to Bottom):
• Filters: Located at top of page• Count of Distinct Instances: Displayed at top• Repave-Specific Filters: Below general filters• Confluence Page Link: Contains all information from presentation plus additional detail• Instance Age Filters:
• Compliant/Non-Compliant options• Text description for why compliant or non-compliant• Extra information not in prior dashboard versions• Source Image Filters:
• Shows "quite a few unknowns" (working to reduce)• Source image based compliance reason field• Longer list of possible values than instance ageCompliance Reason Text - "Inferred" Designation
• When appears: Image name used to infer image date• Clearly highlights inference vs. metadata-based determination• Provides transparency in compliance reason fieldPatching Information Section
• Shows patch-in-place exceptions count (currently 391 VMs)• Lower dashboard section shows exact exceptions• All 391 scanned by Image Factory• Displays whether patch-in-place VMs compliant or not• Compliance Criteria:
• Patch cycles = 0 AND valid patch-in-place exception = Compliant• Missed patch cycles = Non-CompliantScorecards
• "Pretty straightforward"• Standard compliance metrics displayMain Data Table
Conditional Formatting:
• Based on note in top left• Makes status easier to identify visually• Red values at top coincidental• Other colors exist (green and blue visible when scrolling down)Column Organization (Left to Right):
• Instance information• Project information• Platform (GCP/AWS)• Org space information• Ownership information• Instance age compliance data (with actual instance age)• Image information• Image Age: From metadata• Inferred Image Age: From image name inference• Note: Metadata and inferred values line up well (may differ by ~1 day)• Compliance status (Compliant or Non-Compliant)• Compliance reasonExport Reminder
• All table data exportable• Apply filters before export to avoid unnecessary large datasetsTrending Chart
• Currently shows uptick due to source image logic change (correction of error)• Will be backdated so line appears flat• Backdate not yet completed at time of presentationPIP Compliance Results Table
• Information from Image Factory reports• Columns (scrolling right):
• Valid PIP exception status• Missed patch cycles count• Days until exception expires• PIP compliance status (Compliant/Non-Compliant)Patch-in-Place Exceptions Detail Table
• Shows all currently processed exceptions• JIRA ticket that processed exception• Notes for context• Expiry date (available by scrolling right - not shown in recording)• Current state: All same date (will change after ISM review)Legacy Compliance Logic View (TEMPORARY)
• Based on instance age only (old methodology)• Included for comparison purposes• Allows users to look back at previous logic• Removal Timeline: Same time as deprecated dashboard removal (~Feb 20)• Enables comparison between old and new approach• No "Unknown" status in legacy view (only Compliant/Non-Compliant)### Service Account Key Compliance Use Case
Changes from Previous System (Simpler than Repave)
• Dashboard Deprecation:
• Looker Studio dashboard deprecated• Note should already be present; if not, up by Monday• Data Consolidation:
• Combines M com and non-M com data• Specific to keys• Previous dashboard: Separate views• Inactive Keys Inclusion:
• Now includes inactive keys• Previous dashboard: Active keys only• Key status included in dashboard• Additional Metadata Values:
• Previous: Limited metadata• New: Additional metadata values added (specific values not detailed)Critical Note: Notification Process Unchanged
• Current process around central action and notification for GCP keys: NO CHANGES• Notifications continue as currently delivered• Central action on GCP continues (up to and including UAT)• Only Difference: Switch to using new report for reporting purposes• Enables retirement of point solution• Rest of process remains exactly as is• Future Changes: Will be communicated if they occurNavigation and Filters
Standard Filters:
• Located at top of dashboard• "Nothing particularly important to call out"Key-Specific Filters:
• Toggle on key age• Search by access key ID• Key Status: New addition (not on old dashboard which showed only active keys)Dashboard Components
Main Data Table
• Export capability (emphasized throughout presentation)• All key data included:
• Key information• Org space information• Application• Product line• ELT information• Value of data combination demonstratedLoad Timestamp
• Visible in table (referenced earlier in presentation)• Enables multiple date selection• Use load date for trending or comparison with previous dates• Supports historical analysisFilter and Export Workflow
• Filters update table dynamically• Recommended approach: Filter first, then export• Avoids exporting full dataset (e.g., 11,000 records)• Export only needed dataTrending Functionality
• Shows current day compared with same data from 28 days previously• Example provided in presentation:
• January 11 (recording date): 5,707 compliant keys• December 14 (28 days prior): 5,777 compliant keys• Minimal change shown• Enables easy month-over-month comparison on same day of monthCompliance Logic (Simple)
• Non-Compliant: Key age > 90 days• Compliant: Key age ≤ 90 days• Much simpler than repave report logic### Feedback and Support Channels
Primary Communication Channel
• Slack: #ask-cmdb or #cmdb-support• Recommended as first place for questions or comments• Team will direct to different location if more information neededIn-Dashboard Feedback Link
• Located in top left of "pretty much every page"• Link text: "Report an issue or suggest an improvement"• Directs to Microsoft MS Form• Does not spawn ticket (important limitation)• Use Case: Spot issue on dashboard without navigating to JIRA• Direct feedback from dashboard• Review Cadence: Form reviewed once per week• Not for urgent issues• Good method for general feedbackJira Service Desk
• RQM project• Service requests available• Types: Bug, feature request, general support• "Pretty generic" at this stage• May be refined based on volume and type of requests received• Team may route Slack questions to ticket for:
• Questions requiring more information• Need for formal recordConfluence End User Space
• Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)• Covers both use cases from today's presentation• General navigation guidance• Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)• Launch Collateral:
• Link provided for yesterday's session• Will be reposted after this session concludes• Contains recording from yesterday• Host of other reference materials• Chatbot Integration: All content added to Confluence chatbot for instant responses### Future Roadmap
Immediate: Looker Reg Migration
• About to start migration to Looker Reg• Primary Change: URL change• Possible: Slight look and feel changes• Unchanged Elements:
• Logic used• Use cases covered• Core functionality• Communication: New URL communicated for bookmark updatesQ1/Q2 2026 Goals
• 100% Asset Coverage:
• Target: GCP and M com complete coverage• Current: 95% GCP, 85% AWS• Change Records:
• Ingest change records data• Vulnerability Data:
• Add vulnerability information• CI/CD Artifacts:
• Include CI/CD artifact data• Azure Resources:
• Partner with compute team• Build out Azure resource coverageBeyond Q2 - Long-Term Vision
• "A lot more we want to do"• Work won't stop at end of Q2• Example request: Incidents data (definitely want to look at)• Strategic Approach:
• Maintain really high data quality with onboarded use cases• Ensure reliable product• Move slowly through use cases• Avoid compromising overall data quality• Prefer steady quality improvements over rapid, lower-quality expansion### Meeting Logistics and Participant Information
Invitation Criteria
• Already had access from earlier testing• Listed as org space leader• Listed as infrastructure lead in cloud metadata• ISM community membership• Additional requests accommodated (relatively small number)Information Cascade and Access
• All content recorded• All collateral shared afterwards• Standard operating procedures available• Links provided at end of callQuestion Handling
• Request to post questions in chat• Follow-up provided afterwards• FAQ page maintained and updated• Time constraint: 30 minutes of material (emphasis on time limit)• Priority: Get through all content due to important changes• Questions addressed after presentationPresentation Scope Clarification
• Not a technical deep dive• Not covering architecture• Not covering pipelines• Not covering "plumbing underneath the solution"• End user focus• Technical Deep Dives Available: Upon request (reach out to John)• Can facilitate follow-up technical sessions## 2. DECISIONS MADE### System Design Decisions
Decision: Implement Combined Compliance Logic (Instance Age + Source Image Age)
• Resolution: Compliance determined by both instance age and source image age• Previous State: Instance age only• Background: Old logic missed security gap - brand new instances with old source images showed compliant• Business Justification: Better reflects actual security posture• Technical Impact: Introduction of "Unknown" compliance status for indeterminable source images• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team decision)Decision: Introduce "Unknown" Compliance Status
• Resolution: Third status value when source image age cannot be determined• Specific Values: Compliant, Non-Compliant, Unknown• Background: Various reasons make source image age determination difficult• Business Justification: Transparency about confidence in compliance assessment• Mitigation Strategy: Team working to burn through unknown list over time• Important Caveat: Non-compliant instance age overrides unknown image age (results in non-compliant overall)• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Three-Tier Approach for Source Image Age Determination
• Resolution: Use multiple methods in priority order:
• Source image metadata (preferred)• Pattern match from Image Factory• Inferred from image age using timestamp• Technical Implementation: "Inferred" designation in compliance reason when timestamp-based method used• Background: Not all source images have complete metadata• Business Justification: Maximize coverage while maintaining transparency• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Image Age Compliance Thresholds
• Resolution:
• Windows: 2 cycles of image age• Linux/Other: 2 cycles of image age (90 or 180 days)• Background: Different OS types have different patching cadences• Business Justification: Aligns with security policies and patching schedules• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Maintain Legacy Compliance View Temporarily
• Resolution: Include instance-age-only view for comparison• Duration: Until approximately February 20 (when legacy dashboard removed)• Background: Allow users to compare old vs. new methodology• Business Justification: Ease transition, provide context for compliance changes• Temporary Nature: Will be removed with legacy dashboard• Approved by: John FranklinDecision: Consolidate AWS and GCP Data in Single Dashboard
• Resolution: Single table combining AWS M com, AWS non-M com, and GCP instance data• Previous State: Switching between views or no automated feed• Business Justification: Unified view, easier navigation, comprehensive visibility• Applies to: Both repave and service account key use cases• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Include Inactive Keys in Service Account Dashboard
• Resolution: Dashboard shows both active and inactive keys• Previous State: Active keys only• Business Justification: Complete inventory visibility• Technical Implementation: Key status added as filter• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Default Date Range to Last Completed Day
• Resolution: Dashboards default to prior completed day, not current day• Background: Ensures data present (avoids empty tables and null values)• Example: Video filmed on 12th defaults to 11th• User Benefit: Guaranteed data availability• User Option: Can manually update to current day for fresher data (especially US users later in day)• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)### Technical Implementation Decisions
Decision: Use Totality Identity for Access (Not Solutions Identity)
• Resolution: Dashboard access via Totality ID• Previous State: Some pre-existing Looker Studio dashboards required Solutions identity• Background: Simplify access, remove need for GCP console credentials• Business Justification: Broader accessibility, simpler user experience• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: AD Group-Based Access Only (No Direct Access)
• Resolution: Access exclusively through Active Directory group membership• Technical Implementation: Part of ILM processes• Governance: All access governed through group membership• Process: Users must request via Savient → Cloud Group Access• "Request Access" Link: Do not use (explicitly instructed)• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Daily Pipeline Execution
• Resolution: Pipelines run daily• Previous State: Weekly updates for repave dashboard• Background: Frequently requested by users• Exception: Software assets on different cadence• Business Justification: More timely data, faster issue identification• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: 24-48 Hour Propagation Window Acceptable
• Resolution: Up to 24 hours for change propagation (can extend to 48 hours total)• Examples: Destroy and rebuild VM• Support Threshold: Engage support if no change after 72 hours• Background: Depends on exact time of day change made• User Guidance: Wait additional 24 hours before escalating• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Formalize Patch-in-Place Exception Process
• Resolution: Structured process replacing ad hoc approach• Components:
• Jira-based workflow• ISM approval required• BigQuery table updates• CMDB visualization• Previous State: Ad hoc, altercated information• Business Justification: Transparency, auditability, standardization, complete visibility• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: 40-Day Validity for Carried-Over PIP Exceptions
• Resolution: Import existing patch-in-place exceptions with ~40 day validity• Review Process: With ISM during 40-day period• Possible Actions: Reinstate, add new, allow expiration• Current State: Exactly same information as legacy Looker Studio dashboard• Background: Provide continuity during transition• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Ingest Patching Information from Image Factory
• Resolution: Ingest GCP-only patching data• Sources: Combination of Qualys data + source image data• Metrics: Missed patch cycles per VM (0, 1, or more)• Current Coverage: ~400 VMs being scanned• Background: First time this data integrated into compliance dashboard• Business Justification: Complete compliance picture for patch-in-place exceptions• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Backdate Compliance Logic Change in Trending
• Resolution: Backdate new source image logic to historical data• Impact: Removes artificial uptick from logic change• Expected Result: Trending line will appear flat• Status at Presentation: Not yet completed• Business Justification: Accurate historical trending, prevents misleading interpretation• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Migrate to Looker Reg
• Resolution: Move from Looker Studio to Looker Reg• Timing: "About to start migration"• Primary Change: URL change (bookmarks need updating)• Possible Changes: Slight look and feel modifications• Unchanged: Logic, use cases covered, core functionality• Communication Plan: New URL communicated when ready• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Deprecate Legacy Dashboards with 4-5 Week Sunset
• Resolution:
• Banner on legacy dashboards (Monday following presentation)• Keep available for 4-5 weeks• Remove approximately February 20• Assumption: No issues raised during transition• Strong Recommendation: Switch immediately despite extended availability• Communication: Banner contains deprecation notice, link, and instructions• Approved by: John FranklinDecision: Maintain Unchanged Service Account Key Notification Process
• Resolution: Current process for central action and notification completely unchanged• Elements Unchanged:
• Notification delivery• Central action on GCP (up to and including UAT)• Timing• Scope• Only Change: Data source switches to new report• Background: Existing process works well; enables point solution retirement• Future: Changes will be communicated if they occur• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Service Account Key Compliance Logic
• Resolution: Simple 90-day threshold
• Non-Compliant: Key age > 90 days• Compliant: Key age ≤ 90 days• No Change: Logic consistent with previous dashboard• Note: Much simpler than repave compliance logic• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)### Data Architecture Decisions
Decision: Add Load Timestamp to All Tables
• Resolution: Include load timestamp in all data tables• Purpose: Enable historical trending and day-over-day analysis• Use Case: Select multiple date ranges and use timestamp to identify changes• User Benefit: Perform trending in Excel without pre-built charts• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: AWS Non-M com Asset Ingestion
• Resolution: Ingest compute resources from specific AWS non-M com accounts• Scope: Specific accounts/areas (displayed on screen in presentation)• Background: First time this data commingled in automated way• Business Justification: Comprehensive visibility across M com and non-M com assets• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team)Decision: Target 100% Asset Coverage for GCP and M com
• Resolution: Work towards complete coverage• Current State: ~95% GCP, ~85% AWS M com• Timeline: Q1/Q2 2026• Approved by: John FranklinDecision: Q1/Q2 Additions - Four New Data Sources
• Resolution: Add following data sources:
• Change records• Vulnerability data• CI/CD artifacts• Azure resources (partnering with compute team)• Timeline: Q1 and/or Q2 2026• Approved by: John FranklinDecision: Phased Approach to Use Case Expansion
• Resolution: Move slowly through use cases, prioritizing data quality• Rationale: Maintain really high data quality with onboarded use cases• Approach: Ensure reliable product before expanding• Alternative Rejected: Rapid expansion potentially compromising quality• Example Future Use Case: Incidents data (on wish list)• Philosophy: Work won't stop at Q2 end, but quality prioritized over speed• Approved by: John Franklin (CMDB team strategy)## 3. OPEN QUESTIONS### Source Image Age Determination
Question: What is timeline and methodology for reducing unknown compliance statuses?
• Status: Acknowledged as ongoing effort, no specific timeline provided• Current State: Various reasons make source image age determination difficult• Commitment: Team working to burn through unknown list• Information Needed:
• Specific reduction targets• Timeline milestones• Methodology by image type• Expected steady-state percentage of unknowns• Interim Assumption: Unknown status acceptable for sources lacking metadata• Priority: High (affects compliance reporting accuracy)### Patch-in-Place Exception Review
Question: What specific criteria will be used in ISM review of PIP exceptions?
• Status: Review process confirmed for 40-day period, criteria not detailed• Information Needed:
• Review criteria and standards• Documentation requirements• Approval thresholds• Communication plan for changes• Timeline: Next 40 days from presentation• Expected Actions: Reinstate, add new, allow expiration• Responsible Parties: CMDB team + ISM teams### Looker Reg Migration Details
Question: What specific look and feel differences will exist in Looker Reg?
• Status: Migration about to start, details not provided• Confirmed: URL change required• Mentioned: "Slight look and feel changes" possible• Information Needed:
• Specific UI differences• Feature changes or additions• User training requirements• Migration timeline• Testing approach• Communication Plan: New URL communicated when migration complete### Azure Coverage Specifics
Question: What is complete scope and timeline for Azure resource coverage?
• Status: Planned for Q1/Q2, specifics not detailed• Confirmed: Partnering with compute team• Information Needed:
• Specific resource types to be covered• Geographic scope• Coverage percentage targets• Q1 vs Q2 distinction• Dependencies and prerequisites• Priority: Medium (future quarter work)### Incidents Use Case
Question: When will incidents data be added to CMDB?
• Status: Mentioned as example of future use case, no timeline• John's Comment: "Definitely want to look at"• Information Needed:
• Timeline estimate• Scope definition• Data sources• Integration approach• Context: Part of "a lot more we want to do"• Priority: Not determined (post-Q2 consideration)### Trending Chart Backdate
Question: When will compliance trending chart backdate be completed?
• Status: In progress, not yet completed at presentation time• Purpose: Remove artificial uptick from source image logic correction• Expected Result: Line will appear flat• Information Needed:
• Completion timeline• Communication plan when done• Historical data extent• User Impact: Current trending shows misleading uptick• Priority: Medium (data quality improvement)### Change Record Integration
Question: What sources and scope for change records data?
• Status: Planned for Q1/Q2, details not provided• Information Needed:
• Source systems• Data elements to be captured• Relationships to other entities• Use cases enabled• Timeline: Q1 or Q2 2026• Priority: Medium (future quarter work)### Vulnerability Data Integration
Question: What vulnerability data sources and coverage planned?
• Status: Planned for Q1/Q2, details not provided• Information Needed:
• Vulnerability scanning tool sources• Asset types covered• Severity classifications• Refresh frequency• Timeline: Q1 or Q2 2026• Priority: Medium (future quarter work)### CI/CD Artifacts Coverage
Question: What CI/CD platforms and artifact types will be covered?
• Status: Planned for Q1/Q2, details not provided• Information Needed:
• CI/CD platforms in scope• Artifact metadata captured• Relationships to applications/projects• Use cases enabled• Timeline: Q1 or Q2 2026• Priority: Medium (future quarter work)## 4. ACTION ITEMS### Immediate Priority - Dashboard Access and Transition
Action Item: Verify Dashboard Access
• Responsible: All meeting attendees (self-service)• Deliverable:
• Click landing page link provided in chat• Use Totality identity (not Solutions identity)• Verify access granted via AD group• If access denied, request via Savient → Cloud Group Access• Do NOT click "Request Access" in Looker Studio• Timeline: Immediately following meeting• Priority: Critical• Dependencies: AD group pre-membership (95% should have access)Action Item: Migrate to New Dashboards
• Responsible: All users (self-directed)• Deliverable:
• Begin using new CMDB dashboards immediately• Stop using legacy Looker Studio dashboards• Update bookmarks and documentation• Update team runbooks• Timeline: Immediately (despite 4-5 week grace period)• Priority: High• Hard Deadline: ~February 20 (legacy dashboard removal)• Rationale: New compliance logic not in old dashboardAction Item: Add Deprecation Banner to Legacy Dashboards
• Responsible: John Franklin / CMDB Team• Deliverable:

pages.title: Vision Board Search
pages.content:
# Overview:
This is a Multi‑Image Search feature that lets users select multiple images from different listings (across search results), and use them together to perform a more accurate and personalized visual search. Instead of the limited ability to do a search using only a single image in Search similar look, this enhances the experience by allowing users to easily select listing images to express exactly what they’re looking for.# Customer Segment (VOM, VOC,VOBA):
VOC and VOBA# Personas:
• The Homebuyer Persona seeks to combine multiple inspirations into a single, precise search with minimal effort.• The agent persona is looking to translate their client’s needs into visual interpretations and actionable searches.#
Objective:
• Enable richer visual expression: Allow users to communicate complex preferences by selecting multiple images that collectively represent what they want. consumers tend to think in visual combinations, not single images• Increase search accuracy with reduced effort: Reduce ambiguity by blending visual cues (color, shape, style, texture) from several images into a more precise search signal.• Improve discovery relevance: Deliver results that better match user intent by interpreting multiple reference points instead of a single image.• Boost engagement across listings: A discovery experience that feels intuitive and fun. Collecting images across listings creates a mood‑board‑like flow that encourages exploration.• Support iterative refinement: Give users the ability to add/remove images and instantly update results, creating a dynamic and interactive search loop.• Increase conversion and satisfaction: When results align more closely with what users actually want, they feel understood and supported thus improving search confidence.• Differentiation through advanced AI capabilities Multi‑image search positions OneHome as a leader in next‑gen visual discovery.# Critical to Quality:
https://corelogic-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/personal/sobasi_cotality_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B6516AE15-8468-431D-9F25-4DC962D3F897%7D&file=Drawing%204.vsdx&action=edit&mobileredirect=true&wdPreviousSession=7a03a969-cbd9-49bc-9601-942081a0fc04&wdNewAndOpenCt=1768367773340&wdo=4&wdOrigin=wacFileNew&wdPreviousCorrelation=14978d39-b1c1-4672-b005-fa5a7af3142d&wdnd=1&or=PrevEdit (https://corelogic-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/personal/sobasi_cotality_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B6516AE15-8468-431D-9F25-4DC962D3F897%7D&file=Drawing%204.vsdx&action=edit&mobileredirect=true&wdPreviousSession=7a03a969-cbd9-49bc-9601-942081a0fc04&wdNewAndOpenCt=1768367773340&wdo=4&wdOrigin=wacFileNew&wdPreviousCorrelation=14978d39-b1c1-4672-b005-fa5a7af3142d&wdnd=1&or=PrevEdit)# Quant & Qual Analysis & Insights:
What is our data telling us? Quantitatively and Qualitatively
What is the outcome of our competitor analysis?# Business Case:
• No major real estate portal today offers multi‑image blended search. A few competitors have single‑image search, but none allow users to combine multiple images into a composite search intent.This feature positions OneHome as:
• First‑to‑market in visual search innovation• A leader in AI‑powered discovery• A differentiated alternative to Zillow/Redfin/Realtor etc2. Every image a user selects is a signal for preferred layouts, finishes, architectural styles, room types, renovation preferences etc
Multi‑image search becomes a powerful engine for:
• Personalized recommendations• Audience segmentation• Ad targeting and relevance scoring# Proposition:
Using CoreAI .#
Success Metrics/KPIs/OKRs### *What are the success metrics that indicate you’re achieving your goal?
Agent<>Consumer Collaboration
• Lift in conversion from visual search: increase in favourites.• Increase in repeat use of the featureAgent Productivity
• increase in Agent picks (recommendations)Consumer Engagement & Adoption
• Increased search engagement rate Growth in users who initiate a visual search session after interacting with listing images.• Higher multi‑image search activation Percentage of users who select more than one image and complete a multi‑image search.• increase session time#
High-Level Requirements### Requirements
Used to capture initial business requirements for design & solutioning
Team
Requirement
Note
Design Considerations
Solution ConsiderationsAs a user, I want to select images from any listing so that I can build a visual set that reflects what I’m looking for.
As a user, I want a persistent “tray” that stores my selected images so that I can continue browsing without losing my selections.
As a user, I want the ability to add and / or remove selected images so that I can refine the visual inputs for my search.
As a user, I want clear indicators showing which images I’ve selected so that I always know what’s included in my search set.
As a user, I want the system to analyze visual features from each selected image so that the search understands the attributes I care about.
As a user, I want the system to combine multiple images into a single search query so that results reflect the full range of my preferences.As a user, I want results ranked by similarity to my combined images so that I see the most relevant items first.
As a user, I want the system to support multiple images without slowing down so that the experience remains smooth.
As a user, I want the tray to update instantly when I add or remove images so that I feel in control of the search.
As a new user, I want helpful guidance when no images are selected so that I understand how to start a multi‑image search.### Questions / Assumptions / Open Issues

pages.title: New Feature Highlight Enhancements & OH Mobile App Banners
pages.content:
# Overview:
• New Feature Highlights are in-product pop-ups designed to drive awareness, engagement, and adoption of newly launched features. This enhancement initiative includes a redesign of the existing system, the application of logic and user journey logic, configurability by MLS, and the introduction of new highlights for strategic feature launches. ALSO• This feature introduces in-app banners on the mobile web version of OneHome to prompt users to download the OneHome native mobile app, aiming to drive higher download/install rates, boost engagement, and increase retention across mobile platforms.# Customer Segment (VOM, VOC,VOBA):
VOC - Home buyers and renters want a seamless mobile experience with timely, non-intrusive prompts that highlight new features and banners to encourage app download.
VOBA - Real estate Agents want a smooth mobile and web experience for their clients, with well-timed, gentle prompts and banners that highlight features and encourage app downloads#
Objective:
• Streamline the delivery of new feature messaging by enabling MLS-level configurability for Welcome Cards, New Feature Highlights (NFH), and Mobile App Banners, allowing each to be turned on or off per MLS• Improve user engagement with new features through targeted, timely, and relevant in-product highlights, Ensure highlights support the adoption of core product enhancements, increasing feature utilization and satisfaction• Deprecate and replace outdated or irrelevant highlights with a scalable, user-centric system• Increase mobile app installs from qualified OneHome users# Critical to Quality:
• Timeliness: Highlights must appear during relevant moments in the user's journey.• Relevance: Display only to users who can take action or benefit from the feature.• Non-disruptiveness: Must not interrupt core workflows.• Configurability: Must be easily managed at the MLS level.• Design Consistency: Must match OneHome’s brand and UX standards.• Scalability: Ability to support new feature additions without too much dev effort.# Quant & Qual Analysis & Insights:
Qualitative:
• Agent/user feedback highlights confusion around when/why pop-ups appear.• Training and marketing teams report difficulty aligning campaigns with in-product messaging.• Current highlights feel "generic" and are dismissed quickly by experienced users.Quantitative:
• To track how users interact with NFH pop-ups and welcome cards (impressions, clicks, dismissals)• Dismissal Rate: The percentage of users who close or dismiss the pop-up without interacting with it.• Click-Through Rate (CTR): The percentage of users who click on the pop-up compared to the total number of users who saw it.• Time on Page after Pop-up: The average time a user spends on the page after the pop-up is shown.https://mixpanel.com/project/2175557/view/39465/app/boards#id=10461719 (https://mixpanel.com/project/2175557/view/39465/app/boards#id=10461719)
Total Events:
• The welcome popup was shown ~4.66 million times• This includes all triggers, even if the same user saw it multiple times.Unique Users:
• About 1.9 million users saw the popup at least once during this time frame.On average, each user saw the popup about 2.45 times: (In the last 30 days)
• The popup is being shown on multiple visits or sessions per user• It might be shown again after refresh, logout/login, or app reopen# Competitor Research:
Banners to download the Mobile App from Stores
NFH for Mobile App
QR Code to download the App# Business Case:
Improved in-product education and guidance will lead to:
• Higher feature adoption rates, leading to better retention and satisfaction.• Reduction in support queries tied to new features.• Stronger alignment with product marketing, training, and MLS go-to-market strategies.• Competitive differentiation by showcasing innovation (e.g., AI Staging, NLS) in an intuitive, contextual manner.Proposition:#
Success Metrics/KPIs/OKRs
• Achieve a 30% engagement rate with highlighted features• Achieve a Net Positive User Feedback Score on pop-up design (via in-app thumbs up/down or survey)• Track banner impressions - Count of users who were shown on the banner• Install conversion rates - % of banner-click users who installed the app• Banner Clicks (CTR) - Count and % of users who tapped the banner CTA• App store Redirects - Count of users redirected to App / Play store• Drop-off Rate - % of users who dismissed or ignored banner repeatedly#
High-Level Requirements
• Redesign UI of pop-ups for improved readability, aesthetic appeal, and click-throughs.• Journey-Based Targeting Logic to determine when and to whom the highlight appears.• Deprecation Framework to automatically retire old pop-ups after relevance expires.• MLS Configuration Tooling for toggling feature highlights per market/client.• Analytics Layer to track impressions, clicks, dismissals, and conversions.• Feature-Specific Messaging for the following:
• AI Virtual Staging• OneHome Owner “Claim My Home”• Natural Language Search (NLS)• Property and Market Insights• Neighborhood Insights• Search Enhancements• Estimated Cost of Financing Module (ECFM)Requirement
Note
Design Consideration
Solution Consideration
LOE
Phase 1
NFH should be config per MLS.As an user,
I need to configure (enable/disable) which NFHs are shown by MLS,
so I can give them only the information that matters, ensuring relevancy and maximizing feature adoption.
Applies to both Agent and Consumer PortalSeparate FF for both web and Mobile App
Example (AI Image Search):
Campaign active

NFH Should be config per featureAs an user,
I want to configure (enable/disable) visibility for which new feature announcements/ highlights (NFH),
so that I can control whether a particular feature announcement is shown to users at allApplies to both Agent and Consumer Portal
NFH Campaign Control per Feature
As a user,
I want to control the lifecycle of each NFH (start, end, pause, resume),
so that I can run time-bound campaigns for selected featuresAcceptance Criteria:1. User can turn a feature highlight ON/OFF globally.2. Campaign start and end dates can be configured for each feature.
3. Users only see the highlight if:
- The feature is ON.
- Current date is within campaign start/end dates.
- Their MLS is included in the MLS list (Story #1).
- Ability to restart a campaign with a new MLS list or new datesDefault behavior: If campaign dates are not set, the feature follows per-MLS visibility.

Configure New Feature Highlights to display once per UserAs a user,
I only see new feature highlights once
so that users can prevent viewing repeated displays of feature highlights for a better user experience.
• The versioning logic must ensure the onboarding screen is shown only once per major update.• Analytics must track engagement with the updated onboarding (e.g., completed, skipped).
Applies to both Agent and Consumer Portal
### NFH Control must be applicable for welcome cardsApplies to both Agent and Consumer Portal
NFH must be applicable for Mobile App Banners similar to NFH
As an user ,
I want the ability to configure the visibility of New Feature Highlights and mobile app banners per MLS,
so that only relevant or approved features are promoted in specific MLS.
Applies to both Agent and Consumer Portal
Smart "Download/Open App" Banner Based on Installation Status
User not installed the App
As a mobile web user who has not installed the app, I want to see a smart banner prompting me to download the app, so I can easily transition to the full app experience via the App Store or Google Play.
• A Download App Banner appears at the top/bottom of the mobile browser view.• The banner includes:
• App icon, name, and short description• Platform-specific• CTA button: “Open App”• Tapping “Open App” triggers a deep link to open the app directly.• Works across major mobile browsers (Safari, Chrome).• Redirects to the correct app store based on device OS.Support deferred deep links for specific pages (e.g., property details, saved searches) after install
• The app should handle:
• Authenticated and unauthenticated users properly (preserve link context).• Applies
• On tapping “View properties” in email , the user must be taken to the Mobile Web experience, where the banner must be displayed at the top• “View Properties → Mweb experience → Banner shown at the top → Redirects to Google/App store → Handle Auth/UnUath redirects
App Already installed
As a mobile web user who already has the app installed, I want to see a banner prompting me to open the app, so I can continue my journey seamlessly in the native environment.
### Acceptance Criteria:
• When the app is detected as installed on the user’s device, a banner appears on the mobile web experience with:
• App icon and name (e.g., OneHome)• CTA button: “Open App”• Tapping “Open App” triggers a deep link to open the app directly.• Triggers app deep linking to resume or continue user journey within the app (if possible).• Works across major mobile browsers (Safari, Chrome).• Supports Universal Links (iOS) and App Links (Android) for routing.Support custom deep links for specific pages (e.g., property details, saved searches)
• The app should handle:
• Authenticated and unauthenticated users properly (preserve link context).• On tapping
Promotional Email Banner to Drive App Downloads or Engagement
As a user who has not installed the app, I want the email banner to include a download CTA linking to the appropriate app store for my device.
### Acceptance Criteria:
• The email banner should appear at the top or bottom of the email layout (placement configurable).• Banner design includes:
• App icon and name• CTA buttons:
• Download on the App Store• Get it on Google Play• CTA buttons link to platform-specific destinations using smart URLs
• Detect the user’s device and route to the App Store, Google Play, or fallback web page• The banner should be mobile-responsive and render correctly across major email clients (Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail, etc.)• Applies for Consumer Portal• Support deferred deep linking to open a specific page in the app after install• The app should handle:
• Authenticated and unauthenticated users properly (preserve link context).• On tapping Download
### App Already Installed:
As a user who already has the app, I want the email banner CTA to support opening the app directly via deep linking, or redirect me to the correct fallback if that’s not possible.
• When the user clicks the email banner CTA:
• The banner link should use a smart/app link or deep link that attempts to open the app directly.• The app should handle:
• Authenticated and unauthenticated users properly (preserve link context).• If deep linking fails (e.g., unsupported email client or OS restriction), fallback to:
• The mobile web version of the destination page, or• A generic landing page.• The app should handle:
• Authenticated and unauthenticated users properly (preserve link context).

(Usecase 1,2 and 3) (https://www.figma.com/design/jUzTs37VQ2cgt3xuxqfpyf/OneHome-Mobile-App?node-id=6955-15311&p=f&t=AfQ1ox7mHt1fLvoT-0)Store → App Installed → UnAuth Experience
Installed
• View Properties / Property cards2. Download Banner
Redirects to the specific experience (saved searches/specific PRD page).
Handles authenticated and unauthenticated redirects (Usecase 1,2 and 3) (https://www.figma.com/design/jUzTs37VQ2cgt3xuxqfpyf/OneHome-Mobile-App?node-id=6955-15311&p=f&t=AfQ1ox7mHt1fLvoT-0)based on the app experience.(If the app is signed in, it directs you to the sign-in experience, and vice versa).Redirects the user to the New search Experience (Handles Auth / UnAuth Experience### Questions / Assumptions / Open Issues
• What events define a user’s “moment” in the journey for each feature (e.g., search initiated, listing viewed, etc.)?• Should we allow users to revisit dismissed highlights later?Assumptions:
• MLSs have varying appetites for self-service configuration.• Existing highlight infrastructure is not flexible enough for future needs.• Not all users need to see all highlights (role-based logic may apply—e.g., agent vs consumer).### Future / Phase Item
Items that are good, but may be considered for the future
Request / Desire
Future Phase
Comment### Q&A / Key Decisions
What are common questions about the product along with the answers you’ve decided? This is a good place to note key decisions.
Decisions
CommentDeep linking from the banner must be supported only if the app can handle both authenticated and unauthenticated redirect flows.
V1.0 support only Authenticated flow not even Authenticated Redirects and deep linking support
So, We have to implement Authenticated and UnAuthenticated Redirects in the First place to include Promotional banners to Download the appHow app handles aunthenticated and unauthenticated session handling in deferred deep inking ??# Design### User Interfaces

pages.title: Broker Affiliates / Brokerlinx
pages.content:
# Business Context
Ability to support a custom Broker experience which can include:
• Broker Branding
• Marketplace Links
• New Feature Highlights
• Analytics
• White/Black listed agents# High Level Requirements
Requirements
Used to capture initial business requirements for design & solutioningRequirement
Note
Design Considerations Solution Considerations
CONFIGURATIONAbility for backend to identify agent belonging to the Broker
Combo of:
• MLS (OSNs: NTR, CAROLINA)
• AgentID
• BrokerID (Determine ID for Fathom)
We can either maintain a list of all AgentIDs or distinguish based on Broker ID
Ability to turn on/off Broker Affiliation for a particular agent Must be able to remove from list and refresh live
Optout is from whole experience (branding and services)
Based on how we configure this, we can add/remove agents from a white/blacklistIf we use MLS + BrokerID combo, we need to create AgentID blacklist
If we use MLS + AgentID combo, we need to maintain a white list of all agentsBRANDINGApply Broker specific Branding
to follow up with on:
• Logo
• Primary Color (Hex code)
• Secondary Color (Hex code)Pre-Configure in the Backend
Backend Overrides any existing branding sent from Prospects (Where OSN=X, AgentID=Y)
Need to get:
• Logo
• Primary Color Hexcode
• Secondary Color HexcodeBased on same config aboveMARKETPLACE / SERVICESMarketplace / Services Main Page New Header "Services"
• Not a dropdown, so there should be no "My Marketplace" or Insurance, Mortgage or Homepros.
CTAs go to external URL in new tab
Newly Styled Marketplace Home
Mention of "Affiliate Link"?
CTAs open in a new tab (box + arrow icon)
Need final design from with sign off from
We need to identify the URLs that each CTA goes toQUESTION:
• Should we pass any info about the user or property address in the URL?• May be a legal issueProperty Detail CTAs CTAs go to external URL in new tab Using existing design for CTA placement
But 3 CTA
• Mortgage CTA: Get Pre-Approved
• Insurance CTA: Get Insurance Quote
• Title CTA: Get Title & Escrow (New icon?)
to confirm with what Icons & Copy to use for each CTA
Implement URLs on Frontend for Quick Solution
All 3 verticals enabled
Overrides any existing marketplace configsWill property status (e.g off market) make a difference as to:
• When the CTA should be shown?
• Any additional info passed to the 3rd party
Should we pass any info about the user or property address in the URL?
• May be a legal issueMonthly Cost Blade CTAs CTAs go to external URL in new tab Current implementation vs new designs and confirm what is needed
Only 2 CTAs
• Mortgage CTA: Get Pre-Approved
• Insurance CTA: Get Insurance Quote
• Title CTA: Get Title & Escrow (New design)
New design already has this (not yet implemented) https://zpl.io/a7eXnOM (https://zpl.io/a7eXnOM)
to confirm with if we need icons & copyImplement URLs on Frontend for Quick Solution
No Backend ConfigNo Rateplug Override on Monthly Cost Blade If Rateplug is enabled for the agent, then do not override with the modified monthly cost bladeCompare
Add Marketplace CTAs within monthly cost section
Do a new design based on current experience
No new designs needed.
We'll clarify with Dev & QA that we just add the CTAs under Interest Rate & Home Insurance
NOTE: we won't break out the sections like in the new design currently
https://zpl.io/V4M1ARM (https://zpl.io/V4M1ARM)Planner Change CTAs to go to appropriate vertical in planner
Review with on which CTAs should change, where they should goAgent Portal Will be shown on the agent portal as well depending on complexityNEW FEATURE HIGHLIGHTSDisplay NFH Where possible 2 NFH Display on:
• Services in Header
• All 3 CTAs in Property Details
Design popups & copy Solution based on Agent DataOFFLINE REDIRECTSEmail from Trigger Event
Problem: When consumer is ready for insurance and title, they are likely not using OneHome anymore.
Solution: Send email to consumer when offer on property is accepted to inform them of these available services.
Dependency: Fathom to provide us with list of consumers having had their offer accepted.
“Congratulations! Your offer has been accepted! Here are the next steps to close on your new home…”
Hyperlinked affiliate logos?
Logos with separate URLs? When does consumer get an offer
Maybe fathom knows
Get list of consumers
We send an email out to the userOTHER CONSIDERATIONSFor Testing Use CML UAT NOTE Agent List May Differ + OSN & OfficeIDsDisclaimer TextNew Terms & ConditionsQuestions

pages.title: New Feature Highlight - Agent Portal
pages.content:
Requirements:
Agent Portal:
• Needs to show the Agent Portal on Agent Pick(Star)• On Property Cards in Browse Page
• On CTA next gallery in Property Details Page
• Need to show the Agent Pick (we want to be consistent in the terminology to ensure good UX) Bar on the Favorites page• Should be triggered only one per agent
• Contacts Page: • Show the search for contact cards
• Show Preview on contact card
• Show search on Contacts Page• Will be shown after Compare(if compare is triggered)Consumer Portal
• Needs to show the Customer Portal on Agent Pick
• On CTA next gallery in Property Details Page• Should be triggered only one per user
• From the second time consumers will be notified through Consumer Portal(Planned Future through Notification enhancement)Flow:
Component Guide: Feature updates - crl006 - AOTF - Zeplin (https://app.zeplin.io/project/5cc73416e0ef892d8b1b48a0/screen/5eaafcfda6b49db5997eeced)
Agent Portal:

pages.title: OneHome Floor Plans
pages.content:
# Overview:
The OneHome Floor Plans feature enhances the home search experience by introducing a structured, intelligent way for consumers to view and navigate property layouts. Delivered as the first implementation within the OneHome Hyperlocal Neighborhood Insights upkeep, Floor Plans feature is prioritized ahead of other neighborhood insights (such as schools, commute, POIs, demographics, and climate risk). By clearly identifying, organizing, and surfacing floor plan media through a dedicated gallery experience and prominent entry points, OneHome enables buyers to better understand how a home lives, not just how it looks. This initiative aligns with OneHome’s mission to provide the most trusted, informative, and collaborative experience for buyers and agents, while responding directly to strong consumer demand for clearer, more transparent listing information.# Status Quo:
• No dedicated Floor Plans experience exists in OneHome today. Users cannot explicitly view or interact with property layouts as a first‑class feature.• Floor plans are treated as standard images. If an agent uploads a floor plan, it appears only as one or more generic images within the photo gallery. OneHome does not currently identify or classify these images as floor plans.• No separation or discoverability. Floor plans are mixed with listing photos, making them difficult for consumers to find and compare. There is no Floor Plans tab, filter, or direct entry point.• Recent Survey: shows that 85% of buyers rate them very/extremely valuable, 95% are more likely to look closer when a plan is present, and 93% say plans help them envision living in the home—making them the highest‑leverage enhancement to ship now. Floor plans also signal professionalism (87% say agents who include them are better at their job) and are increasingly expected as standard (82% believe the industry should require them)# Objective:
• Improve floor plan discoverability and usability: Introduce a clear, OneHome‑native way for consumers to find and view floor plans through a dedicated tab and CTA, ensuring layouts are easy to locate, clearly separated from photos, and accessible without added friction.• Enhance buyer understanding and confidence: Enable consumers to quickly grasp how a home is laid out and envision living in it.• Elevate listing quality and agent credibility: Present floor plans consistently and professionally across listings, reinforcing agent value and attention to detail while aligning OneHome with evolving industry standards where floor plans are increasingly viewed as a baseline expectation.• Increase engagement with shared listings: Drive deeper interaction with listings by providing prominent entry points (Floor Plans tab and hero CTA) that encourage exploration and longer time spent on the page, without requiring registration or activation.# Customer Segment (VOM, VOC,VOBA):
VOC, VOBA and VOM# Personas:
• Listing Agents
Real estate professionals who market and share listings. They benefit from floor plans being recognized and presented as a first‑class listing element, helping listings appear more complete and professional, increasing engagement, and reinforcing credibility with buyers.• Buyers
Consumers actively or passively searching for homes. Floor plans help them quickly understand layout, compare properties, and envision living in the space—reducing uncertainty and increasing confidence early in the decision‑making process.• Buyer Agents
Agents representing buyers who rely on clear, accessible floor plans to guide clients, shortlist suitable properties, and facilitate more informed conversations before in‑person showings.# Critical to Quality:
• Correct floor plan surfacing: Floor plan media must be reliably identified and only appear when valid floor plan data exists, ensuring accurate CTA and tab visibility.• Reliable navigation: Selecting the Floor Plans CTA or tab must immediately display the first floor plan image without errors or latency.• Consistent cross‑device behavior: Floor plan CTAs and gallery interactions must work consistently on desktop and mobile without UI overlap.• Gallery interaction parity: Floor plan images must support the same core interactions as photos (e.g., “Search Similar Look”).• Guest & accessibility support: Floor plans must be fully accessible to non‑registered users and meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards.# Business Case:
As the real estate search experience becomes increasingly competitive, buyers expect listings to be clear, informative, and easy to evaluate. The WAV Group Floor Plan Consumer Interest Survey (2025) shows that floor plans are now a core expectation, not a nice‑to‑have: 85% of buyers find them very or extremely valuable, 95% are more likely to engage with listings that include them, and 82% believe floor plans should be required on all listings.
By introducing a dedicated Floor Plans experience within OneHome, we can:
• Increase consumer engagement by surfacing one of the most in‑demand listing elements, helping buyers better understand layout and envision living in the home (93% report floor plans improve visualization).• Improve agent value and credibility by presenting listings more professionally and consistently, aligning with consumer perception that agents who include floor plans are better at their job (87%).• Differentiate OneHome’s listing experience with a modern, intuitive, and branded approach that goes beyond basic photo galleries and meets evolving consumer expectations.• Lay the groundwork for future growth by establishing structured floor plan handling that enables richer experiences (e.g., interactive plans, 3D walkthroughs) as buyer expectations continue to rise.Overall, investing in Floor Plans delivers immediate, research‑backed impact on engagement, trust, and perceived listing quality while strengthening OneHome’s competitive position.# Success Metrics/KPIs/OKRs
• Agent Productivity
• Time spent by agents engaging with OneHome listing experiences• Number of properties recommended or shared with clients• Consumer Engagement & Adoption
• Distribution and growth of consumer visits to OneHome listing pages• Increased engagement on listings that include floor plans (e.g., views, interactions)• Reduction in consumer complaints related to missing information or poor navigationThese metrics will help validate that floor plans improve clarity, engagement, and collaboration while strengthening OneHome’s value for both agents and buyers.# Competitor Analysis
Floor Plan from Redfin
Placement:
• The “Floor Plans” button is prominently displayed on the property overview page, alongside other media options such as Photos and Street View.Interaction:
• Clicking “Floor Plans” opens a full-screen viewer with a dark background for contrast.• Users can toggle between floors using a dropdown menu (e.g., 1st floor, 2nd floor, 3rd floor).• The floor plan image is zoomable and pannable, allowing detailed inspection of rooms and layout.Visual Context:
• Each floor plan clearly labels rooms (e.g., Kitchen, Living Room, Deck).• Navigation controls are simple and intuitive, with the floor selector positioned at the bottom-left corner.• Attribution and disclaimers (e.g., “Results Reliable But Not Guaranteed”) are displayed for transparency.# High-Level Requirements

pages.title: Brokerlinx 2.0
pages.content:
# Overview:
Phase 1 - MLS Affiliate POC (Liberty Title)
Brokerlinx MLS Affiliate POC establishes a framework for introducing MLS‑level partners and vendors directly into the OneHome ecosystem, beginning with Title Insurance solution. This will provide MLSs with a scalable, standardized method of bringing third‑party services to consumers, while enhancing engagement and expanding value‑added touchpoints across listing detail page, Services tab, and targeted email campaigns. By launching with a single pilot MLS (RIS) and a single vendor (Liberty Title), POC lays the foundation for future expansion into additional MLS partnerships and service categories, supporting organizational goals of improving consumer engagement, increasing lead‑conversion opportunities, and creating a flexible infrastructure for third‑party integrations within OneHome.
Phase 2 - Multi‑tier Affiliate Hierarchy
BrokerLinx 2.0 (Phase 2) expands the Phase 1 POC from a single MLS‑level vendor model into a multi‑tier affiliate hierarchy that supports MLS‑level, broker‑level, and agent‑level affiliate visibility. This phase establishes scale‑ready controls, override rules, and configuration logic necessary for supporting additional MLSs, additional service categories, and more complex branding/partner ecosystems.# Status Quo:
Original Marketplace Problem (Refer to: https://corelogic.atlassian.net/wiki/x/_a2sAg (https://corelogic.atlassian.net/wiki/x/_a2sAg) )
• OneHome Marketplace currently serves 20 MLSs and 33k agents (4%), but adoption remains low.• Brokers prefer to promote their own affiliate service providers (mortgage, insurance, home services), making Marketplace participation challenging.BrokerLinx Primary Goal
• Promotes cotality/MLS level affiliates, broker-owned or broker-affiliated service and also agent level affiliates providers directly to home buyers in OneHome.• Supports multiple categories: mortgage, insurance, title etc.• Strengthens brand presence and boosts service capture rates.What we have done before RIS POC
We've already configured POCs for several brokerages, such as: Fathom Realty, BHHS Stouffer Realty, Allen Tate Realty across different MLSs (Such as CANOPY, West Penn, MLS NOW)
Example: Allen Tate Realty https://portal.onehome.com/en-US/property/aotf~1149517862~CANOPY?token=eyJPU04iOiJDQU5PUFkiLCJ0eXBlIjoiMCIsInNldGlkIjoiMzUyNzQ2MzI0Iiwic2V0a2V5IjoiMjg1IiwiZW1haWwiOiJzZW5vQG1haWxpbmF0b3IuY29tIiwicmVzb3VyY2VpZCI6MCwiYWdlbnRpZCI6ODYwNjgsImxpc3RpbmdpZCI6IjExODk2NDk5OCIsIlZpZXdNb2RlIjoiMSJ9&searchId=new-search&defaultId=f4878088-1e9b-3e96-9a87-885e033bf84f (https://portal.onehome.com/en-US/property/aotf~1149517862~CANOPY?token=eyJPU04iOiJDQU5PUFkiLCJ0eXBlIjoiMCIsInNldGlkIjoiMzUyNzQ2MzI0Iiwic2V0a2V5IjoiMjg1IiwiZW1haWwiOiJzZW5vQG1haWxpbmF0b3IuY29tIiwicmVzb3VyY2VpZCI6MCwiYWdlbnRpZCI6ODYwNjgsImxpc3RpbmdpZCI6IjExODk2NDk5OCIsIlZpZXdNb2RlIjoiMSJ9&searchId=new-search&defaultId=f4878088-1e9b-3e96-9a87-885e033bf84f)
• Brokerage Branding Integrated: The Allen Tate logo appears in the top-left corner of the OneHome interface (as shown in the screenshots), this branding is persistent across property pages and the services tab.• Services Tab Fully Customized for Allen Tate: includes a custom introductory paragraph describing Allen Tate’s service philosophy and three clearly separated service tiles with affiliate logos.What is missing
Before Phase 1 (POC): We don’t yet have MLS‑approved vendors (mortgage, insurance, title, warranty, home pros) that sits above brokerage branding and can be toggled on by the MLS across all its members.
Before Phase 2: Phase 1 added MLS‑level partners but without the full multi‑level control in place.# Objective:### Phase 1 - MLS Affiliate POC (Liberty Title):
Introduce MLS‑level partner services in OneHome: Establish a standardized framework that enables MLSs to surface approved partners and vendors directly within OneHome. POC launches with Title Insurance as the first MLS‑level service, setting the foundation for future partner categories.
Increase consumer engagement with MLS‑approved services: Surface Title Insurance and conversion opportunities within the listing experience (Listing Detail Page and Services tab), providing consumers with relevant services at the right time without disrupting property search workflows.
Enable consistent lead‑generation for MLS partners: Create clear, low‑friction pathways that route consumers to MLS‑approved partner landing pages, allowing MLSs to generate net‑new consumer leads for participating vendors through standardized CTAs and redirects.
Deliver a scalable, repeatable model: Launch POC with a single service and pilot MLS (RIS) while designing the solution to scale seamlessly across additional MLSs and partner services in future phases.### Phase 2 - Multi‑tier Affiliate Hierarchy:
1. Introduce Multi‑Level Affiliate Selection
Support selecting the correct affiliate based on MLS’s chosen configuration.
2. Provide Configurable Control to MLSs
Allow MLSs to choose:
• Whether affiliates are shown at MLS level• Whether agent/broker branding can override affiliate selection3. define Branding Overrides
4. Prepare for Multi‑Vendor Expansion
5. Maintain POC Simplicity While Preparing for Scale# Customer Segment (VOM, VOC, VOBA):
VOM, VOC and VOBA# Personas:
Who are the key personas?### Phase 1 - MLS Affiliate POC (Liberty Title):
MLS Organizations
MLSs seeking a standardized, scalable mechanism to introduce approved partners and vendors within OneHome. Brokerlinx RIS POC allows MLSs in the future to promote preferred services in a controlled, consistent manner that aligns with market needs.
MLS‑Approved Partner Vendors (e.g., Title Insurance Providers)
Service providers approved at the MLS level who want direct access to high‑intent home buyers. Brokerlinx RIS POC gives these partners structured visibility through listing pages and services tabs, enabling lead generation.
Home Buyers
Consumers actively browsing listings in OneHome who are at various stages of the home‑buying journey. As they view property details, these users benefit from timely exposure to MLS‑approved partner services, that are relevant to their transaction stage. Brokerlinx RIS POC targets these consumers with conversion opportunities that do not disrupt their listing‑search experience.
Agents
Agents whose listings are being viewed and shared through OneHome. While agents are not required to take action or manage partner services directly, they benefit from a more complete, end‑to‑end consumer experience that supports buyers beyond property discovery. Brokerlinx RIS POC enables service exposure without increasing agent workload or forcing manual referrals.### Phase 2 - Multi‑tier Affiliate Hierarchy:
MLS Admins
Require ability to enable/disable MLS‑level partner visibility and override logic.
Broker Leadership
Expect broker‑affiliated vendors to appear when MLS allows.
Agents
Need clarity on branding and when their agent-level branding applies.# Critical to Quality:### Phase 1 - MLS Affiliate POC (Liberty Title):
Reliable MLS‑Level Partner Exposure
Ensure MLS‑approved partner services (in POC Title Insurance: Liberty Title Insurance) consistently surface in all intended locations (Right side of listing detail page and Services tab) for eligible users within the pilot MLS, without configuration drift or inconsistent visibility.
Accurate CTA Redirects & Lead Routing
All CTAs must reliably redirect users to the correct vendor‑provided landing pages. Redirects must function without errors, latency, or misrouting, and preserve a seamless transition from OneHome to the partner experience.
Non‑Disruptive Listing Experience
Partner content must integrate natively into the listing experience without interfering with core property discovery workflows. Service tiles and CTAs should feel natural and non‑intrusive.
Guest & Activated User Compatibility
MLS‑level partner content must be accessible to both non‑registered (guest) users and activated OneHome users, without requiring login, activation, or additional steps to view or engage with services.
Consistent Content & Branding
Partner logos and disclaimers must be consistent across devices and placements, adhering to approved MLS guidelines.
Mobile Experience
All partner placements (info tiles, CTAs, services cards, etc.) must be fully optimized for mobile usage, with responsive layouts, clear hierarchy, and tap‑friendly interactions.
Scalability & Reusability
The Phase 1 implementation must support easy extension to additional MLSs and partner services without requiring fundamental redesign or re‑architecture.
Compliance & Accessibility
All partner components must meet accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA), including contrast, keyboard navigation, alt text, and clear labeling, ensuring inclusive access for all users.### Phase 2 Expanded - Multi‑tier Affiliate Hierarchy:
1. Hierarchical Affiliate Determination
Correctly compute affiliate source based on MLS configuration.
2. Override System
Implement revised override logic that is predictable and MLS‑approved.# Business Case:
Today, MLS‑approved partners and vendors (such as Title Insurance providers) lack a consistent, MLS‑level pathway to reach high‑intent consumers within OneHome, resulting in missed engagement and lead‑generation opportunities. By introducing a standardized MLS‑level partner framework through Brokerlinx MLS Affiliate POC, OneHome can:
• Increase consumer engagement by embedding relevant, transaction‑ready services (e.g., Title Insurance: Liberty Title Insurance in POC) directly within the listing experience, reinforcing OneHome as a comprehensive home‑buying platform.• Unlock new MLS‑level revenue opportunities by enabling approved partners to connect with high‑intent consumers through consistent, scalable in‑product placements.• Reduce reliance on broker‑specific configurations by providing MLSs with a unified, controlled mechanism to promote preferred partners without disrupting agent workflows.• Improve partner lead quality and conversion by reaching consumers who are already active in the home‑search process, through contextual CTAs rather than generic marketing channels.• Lay the foundation for future expansion by establishing a repeatable model that can scale to additional MLSs and partner service categories beyond Title Insurance.Business Case (Phase 2 Expanded)
BrokerLinx 2.0 unlocks:
• MLS‑driven partner governance• Multi‑tier affiliate logic supporting regional variation• Ability to scale new services and new MLSs with minimal engineering effort• Support for future branding products# Success Metrics/KPIs/OKRs### Partner Lead Generation
Number of consumer leads successfully delivered to MLS‑approved partners (e.g., Title Insurance) from OneHome listings and the Services tab### Consumer Engagement
Click‑through rates and Engagement rates on Title Insurance info tiles and CTAs across web and mobile experiences.### Platform & MLS Impact
• Scalability readiness
Ability to onboard additional MLSs or partner vendors using the same framework with minimal incremental effort.• Stakeholder adoption signal
Feedback and interest from MLSs and partners indicating readiness for Phase 2 expansion.# High-Level Requirements### Phase 1 - MLS Affiliate POC (Liberty Title)
