pages.title: My Listings Experience (Creating Sharing Listing Experience for Listing Agent on OH + Agent Profile Page Integration + Open House Registration)
pages.content:
# Overview:
The OneHome “My Listings + Open House Registration” feature enhances the agent and consumer experience by replacing the existing Matrix share‑link view with a modern OneHome listing page, accessible without user activation. Agents’ active listings will also be displayed on their OneHome Profile Page, with full listing access for activated users who also can optionally leave their personal information as leads to agents. Additionally, agents can share Open House information with registration form, allowing consumers to submit their information as prospects (no registration required). This initiative aligns with our organizational goal of improving agent productivity, enhancing agent-consumer collaboration, and increasing consumer engagement through seamless digital experiences.# Status Quo:
• Shared listings generated from Matrix open in a Matrix-hosted listing detail page, not within OneHome.• Listing content is displayed with Matrix’s existing layout, including photos, basic property details, tax information, and agent contact info (with limited modern consumer‑friendly design or interactive elements).• Open House management and registration are not supported in the Matrix share‑link flow; consumers cannot submit their information directly through the page.• No listings nor Open houses are displayed on OneHome Agent Profile Page.Current listing sharing example link: https://matrix.aincar.mlsmatrix.com/matrix/shared/cvl8h38Y7g/633OCEANAVENUE (https://matrix.aincar.mlsmatrix.com/matrix/shared/cvl8h38Y7g/633OCEANAVENUE)# Objective:
• Enhance the listing‑share experience: Replace the current Matrix-generated share link with a modern, OneHome-native listing page, providing consumers with a engaging, branded, intuitive, and also mobile‑friendly OneHome viewing experience (viewing this doesn’t require registration or activation). The listing‑share page includes an optional contact form (name, email, and/or phone) that generates leads.• Enable seamless Open House Registration: Provide a separate experience for sharing Open Houses (not tied to listing sharing) that enables agents to collect consumer information via a OneHome‑generated registration module. Open House Registration yields prospects and can support additional optional fields (e.g., budget range, agent representation [Yes/No], buying timeframe, mortgage pre‑qualification). This consolidates open house visibility and registration management and removes the need for fragmented tools.• Improve consumer-to-agent lead capture: Allow consumers to optionally submit their contact details when viewing a shared listing without creating an account, increasing lead volume while keeping friction low.• Generate new leads from listing shares and new prospects from open houses:
Create clear, low‑friction capture points across both experiences:
• Listing shares → leads via the optional contact form.• Open House shares → prospects via the dedicated registration module with enhanced data capture.• Increase agent visibility and credibility: (Scope Updated, this is not included in Phase 1. This is deferred to Phase 2/Future Consideration) Surface all of a listing agent’s active listings and Open houses directly on their OneHome Profile Page, enabling consumers to easily discover an agent’s profile and listings within the OneHome ecosystem.# Customer Segment (VOM, VOC,VOBA):
VOC, VOBA and VOM# Personas:
Who are the key personas?
• Listing Agents
Real estate professionals who create, share, and promote their listings and open houses. They use the listing‑sharing experience to generate leads, and use the Open House Registration experience to generate prospects. They benefit from a streamlined way to share listings and open houses without manual work or third‑party tools.
(Note: Displaying listings or open houses on the Agent profile page is deferred to Phase 2/Future Consideration.)• Open House Attendees (Prospects / High‑Intent)
Consumers actively searching for homes who discover open houses through shared listings or agent profile pages. These users are motivated to attend in‑person events, possibly pre-qualified and willingly provide basic contact information via OneHome’s open house registration module. They represent high‑quality, near‑term prospects for agents and brokerages.• Early‑Stage Home Explorers (Listing‑Share Leads / Passive Intent)
Consumers who explore neighborhoods, price ranges, or property types without immediate buying intent. They typically arrive through shared listing links and browse freely without needing an account. The optional contact form on listing pages creates a lightweight mechanism for capturing leads (name, email, phone only) without imposing registration requirements.• Activated OneHome Users
Consumers who have completed the ECFM activation process. They gain full access to view an agent’s complete listing portfolio directly on the OneHome Agent Profile Page, and further explore same agent’s other listings or open houses. (Note: Viewing an agent’s full listing portfolio or open houses on the Agent profile page is a Phase 2/Future Consideration item, not part of Phase 1.)• Brokerage Offices & Teams
Administrative coordinators, marketing staff, or team assistants who support agents in promoting listings and managing open houses. They benefit from consistent, professional, OneHome‑powered sharing flows that reduce reliance on external forms, spreadsheets, or ad‑hoc processes.# Critical to Quality:
• Seamless Share‑Link Transition
Ensure that all listing share links generated by agents reliably redirect to the OneHome listing detail page without service interruption or dependency on Matrix UI.• Agent Notification & Lead/Prospect Alerts
Agents must receive timely, reliable notifications whenever a new lead (from listing share contact forms) or prospect (from Open House Registration) is submitted. Notifications must clearly identify the source (listing share vs. open house) and include all submitted consumer details. Delivery must occur through supported channels (e.g., email and/or Matrix).• Consistent Listing Availability
Gurantee accurate listing data remain synchronized with MLS data in real-time on both OneHome listing pages and the Agent Profile Page. All active listings for a listing agent must surface consistently and without delay. (Note: Displaying listings on the Agent profile page is deferred to Phase 2/Future Consideration and is not required for Phase 1.)• Open House Registration Reliability
The Open House Registration experience must function consistently whenever an agent shares an Open House. This includes accurate open house indicators and a stable registration module so consumers can submit prospect information without friction or errors.• Lead/Prospect Delivery Accuracy
All contact submissions—whether leads (listing shares) or prospects (open houses)—must be accurately captured and delivered to the listing agent without loss, delay, duplication, or data inconsistency.• Guest Access Compatibility
The OneHome listing page and the Open House Registration module must remain fully accessible to non‑registered and non‑activated users, without prompting for login or account creation.• Mobile‑First Experience
The listing page, agent listing portfolio, and registration module must be optimized for small screens with responsive layouts, easy tap targets, and clear hierarchy.• Accessibility Compliance
All listing pages and registration components must meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards (contrast, keyboard navigation, alt text, field labeling, error states).# Business Case:
As the real estate search experience becomes increasingly competitive, consumers expect high‑quality presentation, ease of use, and seamless pathways to connect with agents. The current Matrix share‑link experience is functional but outdated and has no open house registration experience, also disconnected from the broader OneHome platform. By bringing listing‑sharing and open‑house‑sharing into OneHome, we can:
• Increase consumer engagement with a modern and engaging listing experience that reinforces OneHome’s brand.• Improve agent value and efficiency by centralizing the ability to share listings (lead generation) and share open houses (prospect generation), removing the need for external forms, spreadsheets, or manual registration workflows.• Increase lead and prospect capture opportunities by enabling frictionless contact submission on listing pages (leads) and streamlined registration for open houses (prospects), even for non‑activated consumers.• Increase agent retention by offering a more branded, high‑exposure experience for showcasing their listings and open houses.# Success Metrics/KPIs/OKRs#### Agent<>Consumer Collaboration
• New leads generated via Share My Listing Experience• New Prospects generated via Open House registration• Time/session counts of agent–client interactions facilitated by shared listings or shared open houses.Agent Productivity
• Time spent on My Listings on the Agent Profile Page (Phase 2/Future Consideration)• Number of properties an agent shares or recommends to clients through OneHome workflows.Consumer Engagement & Adoption
• Distribution of consumer visits to OneHome, especially traffic driven by listing‑share links and open‑house‑share links.• Reduction in complaints about missing information and navigation related to shared listing pages or open house registration flows.# Typical Open House Experience and Problem#### Note: Summarized with Zillow, Redfin, Realtor and Homes#### Step 1 — User Views Listing & Open House Details
Platforms show on listing page:
• Open house dates/times• “Add to calendar”#### Step 2 — User Clicks “Add to calendar”
• Triggers a web/embedded form or modal.• Options to add open house as an event on different calendar### Problem - Add to Calendar Only
Agent does NOT receive lead
• User clicks “Add to Calendar”• Calendar event (ICS) is saved locally• No form submission• No contact info sharedAgent is not notified, No lead is created# Typical Lead Generation Experience#### Note: Summarized with Zillow, Redfin, Realtor and Homes#### Step 1 — User Initiates Intent via Engagement CTAs
Common CTAs across platforms include:
• “Ask a question”, “request a private showing” on Realtor• “Contact agent”, “Request a tour” on Zillow• “Request showing” on Redfin• “Send a Message” on HomesThese CTAs signal strong purchase intent and act as the primary lead‑generation entry points.#### Step 2 — User Completes Contact or Registration Form
Selecting a CTA triggers a web‑based or embedded modal form with a structured digital lead‑capture flow.
Standard fields collected across competitors include:
• Name• Email address• Phone numberOptional or advanced qualification fields may include:
• Budget range• Agent representation (Yes / No)• Buying timeframe#### Step 3 — Confirmation
Typical confirmations:
• On‑screen success message• Automated email with event info (if provided with email)#### Step 4 — Agent Receives Lead#### Delivery Methods
• Email notification• SMS alerts (depending on agent settings)• In‑app notifications (Agent dashboard, Inbox / notification center, Lead management tools)# High-Level Requirements

pages.title: Property/Market Stats Enhancement
pages.content:
# Overview:
OneHome plans to enhance its platform by providing detailed property and market statistics on a per-property basis. These insights will include key data such as price trends, Median Home Price in the Area, Inventory Levels/hot areas, Market Appreciation Rates, Neighborhood Demographics and Crime Rates, Proximity to Amenities, Job Market and Economic Growth, Rental Yield and Investment Potential neighborhood comparisons, and property history, accessible to users during their home search.
Additionally, agents will receive specific market insights in the Agent Portal to assist in guiding clients through decision-making, helping users make informed decisions and facilitating quicker deal closures.# Customer Segment (VOM, VOC,VOBA):
• Primary users: Home buyers and renters using OneHome to explore properties and make purchasing or renting decisions.• Secondary users: Real estate agents using the Agent Portal to access market and property insights for their clients.#
Objective:
To provide users with valuable, easy-to-understand property and market data to aid in their decision-making process, and to equip agents with deeper insights through the Agent Portal, ultimately leading to faster, more informed transactions.?# Critical to Quality:
• Property and market statistics must be presented in a clear, user-friendly format that enhances understanding without overwhelming users.• Data should be accurate, regularly updated, and localized to the specific property and its surrounding market.• Insights must be relevant and useful for both buyers and renters, as well as agents in the Agent Portal, with actionable information that can influence decisions.• Ensure data privacy and compliance with any legal or market regulations.# Quant & Qual Analysis & Insights:
Quantitative:
• Measure user engagement with property insights (e.g., clicks on statistics, time spent reviewing data).• Track how often agents access market insights in the Agent Portal and its correlation with closed deals.• Analyze the reduction in time from property viewing to decision-making for users interacting with the new feature.Qualitative:
• Conduct user surveys to gather feedback on the usefulness of the property and market insights.• Interview agents to assess how market insights in the Agent Portal are influencing their client interactions.# Business Case:
Providing deeper property and market insights positions OneHome as a more valuable tool for home buyers, renters, and agents. By empowering users with data, we can improve decision-making efficiency and shorten the time to close deals, increasing overall platform usage and customer satisfaction. Additionally, enhanced agent insights can strengthen the platform’s value for real estate professionals.#
Success Metrics/KPIs/OKRs### Objective: Increase engagement with property insights to help users make more informed decisions.
• Key Results:
• Achieve X% increase in user engagement with property and market data within the first 3 months.• Reduce the average time to close a deal by Y% as a result of improved decision-making.• Increase agent interaction with market insights in the Agent Portal by Z%.• KPIs:
• Click-through rate (CTR) on property and market insights.• User retention rates for those interacting with in-depth statistics.• Conversion rates of users who access market data to finalized deals.• Agent satisfaction scores related to the new insights provided in the Agent Portal.###
High-Level Requirements### Requirements
• Property Detail Pages:
• Display market trends (e.g., price fluctuations, average days on market) alongside individual property details.• Include neighborhood comparison data (e.g., crime rates, schools, average home values) to give users a broader context.• Ensure that data visualizations (e.g., graphs, charts) are easy to interpret and interactive.• Agent Portal:
• Provide agent-specific insights that are not available to end users, such as in-depth property history, market dynamics, and pricing strategy suggestions.• Integrate these insights into the existing Agent Portal without disrupting the current workflow.• Data Sources:
• Ensure data is sourced from reliable and up-to-date databases (e.g., MLS data, third-party market analysis).• Build mechanisms for frequent data updates and automatic synchronization with the latest market changes.### Questions / Assumptions / Open Issues
• What are the specific types of market data and statistics that will be most valuable to users and agents?• What data can we pull from Matrix, EPW etc• Are there any limitations or costs associated with accessing third-party market data sources?• How will we handle discrepancies in data or outdated information?• How frequently will the data need to be updated to ensure relevance?• Will agents have the ability to customize or prioritize certain types of insights based on their needs?# Design### User Interfaces
<Link to Designs>
<Link to Component Guides>
[Applicable Screen Shots for visual reference]#### Things to consider in designs
• Happy State• Empty State (i.e empty list of properties)• Error State (when api to load list fails, what do we show?)• Component guide is referenced as source of truth
• Larger screens will utilize component guide, but may not be 100% accurate### User Journeys
CTQ Experiences mapped through process flows, work with wireframes, and then with design on user flows (assess the need to do so)### Copy Keys
• Ensure the designs are marked up with the copy keys• Ensure copy deck is updated with the copy keys https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1vdmaD_Z6xEY23kyb8cZfBbUTDnAXiFe3LSX2mjpgyOI/edit#gid=271396961 (https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1vdmaD_Z6xEY23kyb8cZfBbUTDnAXiFe3LSX2mjpgyOI/edit#gid=271396961)• If creating a new page in the portal, ensure you name the page with a META copy key (e.g META_TITLE_SEARCH = OneHome™ | Search Properties)# Solution### Flow Diagram

pages.title: OneHome Property and Market Insights
pages.content:
# Overview:##
Status QuoMatrix
Currently reads from individual Matrix Instances and provides insights within Stats and Market Reports. However, does not present any insights from PRD data within Matrix experiences.Realist
Realist currently reads from REAS (SDP), using the market trend API powering the property intelligence dashboard. Listing data is only available through the PIN network participants in SDP, which has a coverage issue (2/3rd counties are serviced). In markets who are not part of the PIN only PRD property data gets presented to the AgentTrestle Insights
Trestle currently reads from Location Inc API as well as is currently building more integrations with SDP data in Q2, However this would still mean that Trestle in markets that are not part of the PIN, would not have access to listing data to power listing and market trends
OneHome
Currently services all its US customers with listing stats and trends such as median sale prices etc. from Data gathered through Liveby made available by CoreLogicOneHomeOwnerCurrently is not able to service any of its clients in the US with listing stats and trends, for Homeowners to be aware of market conditions, and finally encourage them to buy or sell# Objective:The Property and Market Stats initiative will help create the ability to read from a central database to provide Property / Listing Trends or Market Trends in all of the RES product suite (Matrix, MLS-Touch, Realist, Trestle, Clareity, and OneHome in the form of:
• Matrix: Interactive scheduled and custom reports, interactive charts and API/feed (deprecate the use of IE mode in Edge)• Realist: Interactive Widgets and APIs• Trestle Location Insights: APIs to support Listing/Market trends• OneHome: Interactive Widgets and APIs• OneHomeOwner: Interactive Widgets and APIsNotes: Matrix, OneHome, and MLS-Touch to submit requirements to Tim (Demoable by client summit)our goal is to provide users with valuable, easy-to-understand property and market data to aid in their decision-making process, and to equip multiple users such as MLSs, Brokers, agents and consumers with deeper insights through the RES product suite, ultimately leading to faster, more informed transactions.# Customer Segment (VOM, VOC,VOBA):
• Primary users: Brokers / Broker Offices, Agents also home buyers, Home owners and renters using OneHome to explore properties and make purchasing or renting decisions• Secondary users: MLS Members and staffers using Matrix / MLS-Touch to access market and property insights---
TO BE UPDATED# Critical to Quality:
• Property and market statistics must be presented in a clear, user-friendly format that enhances understanding without overwhelming users.• Data should be accurate, regularly updated, and localized to the specific property and its surrounding market.• Insights must be relevant and useful for both buyers and renters, as well as agents in the Agent Portal, with actionable information that can influence decisions.• Ensure data privacy and compliance with any legal or market regulations.# Quant & Qual Analysis & Insights:
Quantitative:
• Realist Property Details Report averages at 7.5 Million reports downloaded per month• Realist Market Trends - https://prd.realist.com/reports/market-trends (https://prd.realist.com/reports/market-trends) is accessed about 50K times/month• Realist is building property intelligence via market trends API (using RES data)• REAS does not have good listing data coverage with a 2-3 month lag• Data Flows between Diablo to Key Stone to Realist (SDP reads from Diablo)Qualitative:
• Conduct user surveys to gather feedback on the usefulness of the property and market insights.• Interview agents to assess how market insights in the Agent Portal are influencing their client interactions# Business Case:
Providing deeper property and market insights positions OneHome as a more valuable tool for home buyers, renters, and agents. By empowering users with data, we can improve decision-making efficiency and shorten the time to close deals, increasing overall platform usage and customer satisfaction. Additionally, enhanced agent insights can strengthen the platform’s value for real estate professionals.#
Success Metrics/KPIs/OKRs### Objective: Increase engagement with property insights to help users make more informed decisions.
• Key Results:
• Achieve X% increase in user engagement with property and market data within the first 3 months.• Reduce the average time to close a deal by Y% as a result of improved decision-making.• Increase agent interaction with market insights in the Agent Portal by Z%.• KPIs:
• Click-through rate (CTR) on property and market insights.• User retention rates for those interacting with in-depth statistics.• Conversion rates of users who access market data to finalized deals.• Agent satisfaction scores related to the new insights provided in the Agent Portal.###
High-Level Requirements### Requirements
• Property Detail Pages:
• Display market trends (e.g., price fluctuations, average days on market) alongside individual property details.• Include neighborhood comparison data (e.g., crime rates, schools, average home values) to give users a broader context.• Ensure that data visualizations (e.g., graphs, charts) are easy to interpret and interactive.• Matrix / MLS-touch / Agent Portal:
• Provide agent-specific insights that are not available to end users, such as in-depth property history, market dynamics, and pricing strategy suggestions.• Integrate these insights into the existing Agent Portal without disrupting the current workflow.• Data Sources:
• SDP - RCSL• LiveBy/NeighborhoodScout• Ensure data is sourced from reliable and up-to-date databases (e.g., MLS data, third-party market analysis).• Build mechanisms for frequent data updates and automatic synchronization with the latest market changes.• Market insights• Agent/broker insights• Inventory insights### Questions / Assumptions / Open Issues
• What are the specific types of market data and statistics that will be most valuable to users and agents?• What data can we pull from Matrix, EPW etc• Are there any limitations or costs associated with accessing third-party market data sources?• How will we handle discrepancies in data or outdated information?• How frequently will the data need to be updated to ensure relevance?• Will agents have the ability to customize or prioritize certain types of insights based on their needs?# Competitor Analysis
• Domus Analytics• Info Sparks• Showtime reportshouse sigma - https://housesigma.com/on/toronto-real-estate/assign225-261-queens-quay-east/home/10QqypNGPxm3LGlV?id_listing=bqB176Z8eGv7ZajD (https://housesigma.com/on/toronto-real-estate/assign225-261-queens-quay-east/home/10QqypNGPxm3LGlV?id_listing=bqB176Z8eGv7ZajD)
Homes (https://www.homes.com/property/246-sumpter-st-brooklyn-ny-unit-3-a/x1fbv5bdzx5ev/).com
FlyHomes - https://flyhomes.com/search/details/217-n-11th-st-1-new-york-city-ny-11211/52335148 (https://flyhomes.com/search/details/217-n-11th-st-1-new-york-city-ny-11211/52335148)# Top OneHome Client Portal Data to Consider:• Market insights
• Listing views/favorites/likes• Median price• New listings/active listings in area• Median days on market• Median sold price vs average days on market• Sold, active and new listings• Sold price distribution• Median rental price• Property type distribution• Price/square foot• Owner Occupied• Renter OccupiedBuyers Agent View:## Property-Specific Stats (For Each Home)
• Listing Price vs. Market Value – Is the home fairly priced based on recent sales?• Price per Square Foot – Compared to neighborhood and city averages.• Days on Market (DOM) – Indicator of demand and pricing strategy.• List-to-Sale Price Ratio – Are homes selling above or below the asking price?• Recent Comparable Sales (Comps) – Price trends of similar homes within the last 6 months.• Appreciation Potential – Projected home value growth based on trends.• Financing & Loan Restrictions – FHA, VA, or conventional loan eligibility.## Market Trends & Demand Metrics
• Buyer vs. Seller Market Conditions – Supply/demand trends.• Absorption Rate – Speed at which homes are selling.• Months of Inventory – Indicator of market competitiveness.• Interest Rate Trends – Impacting affordability and mortgage approvals.• Price Growth Over Time – 1-year, 5-year, and 10-year growth trends.• Multiple Offer Trends – Are bidding wars common?• Cash Buyer vs. Financed Buyer Stats – Understanding competition.# Available Data

pages.title: OneHome Mobile App v1.1.1
pages.content:
OverviewTo evolve the OneHome Mobile App v1.1.1 user experience with deeply integrated, native AI capabilities and critical experiences. This integration aims to reduce latency, ensure a cohesive UI/UX, and empower users to leverage AI insights directly within their existing workflows without context switchingCleaning up Critical Experiences & Revenue Impact: AI Search Similar Look, Roomvo, ISP Ads, OneHomeOwner Integration, Portal Ads, Audience Intelligence + Consent Manager, NLS + Image search, DPR (Encrypted Experience).
App Review Prompt : To Implement a sentiment-filtered review trigger that captures high-intent users at their peak moment to drive the store rating toward a 4.1+ star average.
Feedback Prompt : Deploy an in-app channel to intercept users with unresolved friction points, proactively managing at-risk store reputation signals by resolving issues privately.
Customer Segment: VOC, VOBA and VOM
Personas:
VOC
• Home Buyer: Individuals or families looking to purchase residential properties.• Renter: Individuals seeking residential properties for rent.• Commercial Investor: Businesses or individuals interested in commercial properties.• Land Developer: Individuals or companies in search of land for development projects.• Home Owner: Home Owners can leverage the OneHomeOwner feature on mobileVOBA: Agents using MLS touch
VOM: MLSs currently using the MLS Client App moving over to OneHome Mobile App, also applicable to New MLS customers
Objective
• Creating an agent-branded mobile app experience, made available to the consumer• Help increase collaboration among agents and consumers• Increase stickiness among consumers• Drive ancillary revenue via strategic partner integrationsHigh-Level Requirements
Used to capture initial business requirements for design & solutions
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Q452jofL5vY6UgR6PHZgJVxZscS3PNMirrq-R2Zemh8/edit?gid=2128818458#gid=2128818458 (https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Q452jofL5vY6UgR6PHZgJVxZscS3PNMirrq-R2Zemh8/edit?gid=2128818458#gid=2128818458) Requirement
Notes
Design Consideration
Solution ConsiderationApp Review Prompt
As a satisfied OneHome app user,
I want to be prompted to rate the app only after meaningful and positive engagement,
so that I can share my experience at the right moment without disruption.
Favoriting a Property
System Check
Eligible only if all are True:
• Favorites >=3• App Session >=2 (User must open and use the app on two separate occasions, not within the same continuous page)• No error/Crash in Session• Not Prompted in last 120 dayshttps://developer.android.com/guide/playcore/in-app-review (https://developer.android.com/guide/playcore/in-app-review)
https://developer.apple.com/documentation/storekit/requesting-app-store-reviews (https://developer.apple.com/documentation/storekit/requesting-app-store-reviews)Internal Feedback FormAs a user, I want to deploy an in-app feedback channel that triggers when a user encounters a friction point, So that I can resolve their issues privately and prevent negative sentiment from reaching public app stores or review sites.
As a user, I want to view the critical functionalities in the mobile app with a clean and intuitive UI/UX design so that I can easily navigate and efficiently complete my tasks.
• OneHomeOwner• Portal Ads• Audience Intelligence + Cordless Media• DPR (Encrypted Experience)
As a user, I want to view the AI capabilities Integrated to the App, so that I can leverage AI insights directly
• AI Search Similar Look (Visual Search Suite)• Roomvo• ISP Ads• NLS + Image Searchhttps://www.figma.com/design/jUzTs37VQ2cgt3xuxqfpyf/OneHome-Mobile-App?node-id=5163-27089&t=GxTMBxDexMyAW3yd-1 (https://www.figma.com/design/jUzTs37VQ2cgt3xuxqfpyf/OneHome-Mobile-App?node-id=5163-27089&t=GxTMBxDexMyAW3yd-1)
https://www.figma.com/design/jUzTs37VQ2cgt3xuxqfpyf/OneHome-Mobile-App?node-id=9830-11520&t=GxTMBxDexMyAW3yd-1 (https://www.figma.com/design/jUzTs37VQ2cgt3xuxqfpyf/OneHome-Mobile-App?node-id=9830-11520&t=GxTMBxDexMyAW3yd-1)
https://www.figma.com/design/jUzTs37VQ2cgt3xuxqfpyf/OneHome-Mobile-App?node-id=5608-58183&t=GxTMBxDexMyAW3yd-1 (https://www.figma.com/design/jUzTs37VQ2cgt3xuxqfpyf/OneHome-Mobile-App?node-id=5608-58183&t=GxTMBxDexMyAW3yd-1)
App Review Prompt Scenarios
Prompt scenario
Trigger When
Logic
Design considerations•
Using EMC / AI Image Search
User completes an AI image search and views results or saves a result.
ai_search_completed = true
AND user spends ≥ 10 seconds exploring results
AND no error occurred
If yes•
Favoriting a Property
User favorites 3 or more properties (or favorites their first property, depending on UX decision).
Recommend Trigger Logic
Eligible only if all are True:
• Favorites >=3• App Session >=2 (User must open and use the app on two separate occasions, not within the same continuous page)• No error/Crash in Session• Not Prompted in last 90 daysfavorite_count >= 3
AND user has favorited at least once in the last 7 days
User Action
Day 1
• Installs App• Opens App (Session 1)• Favorites 3 properties• Leaves AppDay 2
• Opens App again• Browses more listings• Views favorites listSystem check
• Favorites >= 3• App Session >=2• No error/Crash in Session• Not Prompted in last 90 days
•
Reaching a Viewing Milestone (e.g., 10 properties viewed)
User views 10 property detail pages.
total_property_detail_views >= 10
AND total app sessions ≥ 2
•
User Interacts With Saved Homes
User views their Saved Homes list 2 times, or edits saved items.
saved_homes_opened >= 2
OR
User removes/organizes saved homes
•
User Adds a Property to Compare
User adds 2 properties to compare OR completes a comparison session.
compare_list_count >= 2
AND compare view open event captured### Questions / Assumptions / Open Issues

pages.title: OneHome Upkeep (UI/UX Enhancements: POI, Commute Scores, Market Stats, Neighborhood Insights, School UI/UX, Climate Risks, Street View, Floor Plans, Information Hierarchy)
pages.content:
# Overview:
OneHome upkeep enriches the home search experience by integrating detailed, neighborhood-level data with modernized UI/UX interface and updated information hierarchy strategy. By surfacing insights such as schools, commute options and scores, lifestyle amenities (POI), demographics, market stats, neighborhood insights, climate risk, and interactive floor plans, we empower homebuyers to evaluate not just the home, but the living experience surrounding it. This upkeep supports OneHome’s mission to deliver the most trusted, informative, and collaborative experience for buyers and agents.# Status Quo:
Current:
• Market stats are basic, limited to tax and sale history for the property (Excluding listing price history) and historical price trends for the neighborhood.• Demographics breakdown exists but limited to age, education, and marital status.• Neighborhood information UI is outdated. (e.g.: school score and commute score)• Information hierarchy strategy and UI is outdated• OneHome lacks immersive visualization features such as Google Street View or 3D Fly-Around (similar to aerial “fly view” in mapping apps), Users cannot visually explore the property’s surroundings beyond static maps and photos.
• Street View exists but is hidden behind pegman‑drag; low discoverability• No floor plan feature currently exists; users cannot view or interact with property layouts• No listing urgency, or engagement (Viewed/Seen)• Outdated Estimated Monthly Cost experiences, also with no indication of the closing cost calculations• Climate risk information is minimal, only spatial flood zone information is hidden under Public Record Data section, no other types of climate risk is provided.Objective:
• Deliver contextual neighborhood intelligence: Provide homebuyers with rich, hyperlocal insights — schools, lifestyle amenities, commute options and scores, climate risk, sunlight exposure, comprehensive demographic information and market trends, enabling more informed, confident, and faster purchase decisions.• Elevate market analytics: Move beyond basic tax history, sale history and historical price trends, to offer dynamic market stats, including property value ranges and neighborhood-level comparisons.• Differentiate OneHome in the market: Offer deeper, personalized real estate insights that go beyond property details, positioning OneHome as the most trusted and innovative home search platform.• Empower clients with actionable insights: Equip agents with comprehensive local market knowledge and interactive tools to strengthen their advisory role and client relationships.• Boost engagement through modern UI/UX: Replace outdated, static modules with visually rich, interactive experiences for schools, lifestyle, commute, and market stats, driving higher engagement and trust.• Provide a realistic sense of the property: Integrate immersive visualization features such as Street-Level View, Fly-Around, and interactive Floor Plans that allow users to explore property layouts by selecting different floors and zooming into details.• Improve property detail clarity through optimized information hierarchy: Restructure property detail pages and monthly payment section to prioritize key buyer needs.# Customer Segment (VOM, VOC,VOBA):
VOC, VOBA and VOM## 2025 Feedback Considerations
Product feature focused
n = 9117 consumers (9,070 “one‑thing to improve” responses received)
• sum of unique theme hits: 2,963 → 32.7% covered under 12 product themes:Core Product Features
Description
ImpactFilters & Search Options - 8.5%
• HOA toggle (48 mentions)• Pet‑friendly filter (46)• Parking / Garage (32)• School/Grade filter (27)• Finished basement (16)• 55+ / Senior filter (17)• Lot/Yard size filter (7)• Price per sq ft (6)• “Add search feature of number of parking spots - a five bedroom with one or zero parking spots is of no interest to me.”• “I need the ability to adjust the settings myself.”• “Filter out HOA homes easily, I refuse to buy an HOA.”The most “one-thing to improve” issue.
HighData Quality & Completeness - 5.3%
• Utilities (well/sewer/gas) (103)• Taxes/assessed value (67)• Acreage/lot size clarity (41)• Price history / DOM (26)• Flood zone info (22)• Sold/Rented price (where allowed) (6)• “SHOW the rented price on rental listings… should show what the property actually rented for.”• “There are quite a few errors, photos don’t match the map; contradictory information.”• “I would put everything that Realtor has - school zones, flood zone, taxes… people want access to more information.”HighMedia & Floorplans - 4.8%
• Floor plans (60)• Video tours (25)• 3D/Virtual tours (22)• Larger/full‑screen photos (9)• Higher resolution photos (4)• “Video.” (Direct request for video tours)• “I would suggest it provides a detailed 3D view of the homes.”• “Larger pictures easier navigation.”MediumNavigation & UI Friction - 3.3%
• Layout/scrolling issues (52)• “Back to list” resets position (11)• Remove pop-ups/welcome gate (6)• Too many clicks / compact table (3)• Mobile photo gallery too small (2)• “When I click a house and go back, it takes me all the way back to the top — very frustrating.”• “Too difficult to navigate — don’t want to fill out information.”• “The interface is not intuitive and feels clunky.” (From “not intuitive” patterns)HighMaps & Geo - 2.7%
• Street View (41)• Satellite view (22)• Parcel/lot boundaries (14)• Improve Google‑like behavior (8)• “Fix the map.” (Direct complaint)• “I miss street view and fly around.”• “Map can be difficult to navigate… could use more options.”MediumPerformance & Stability - 1.9%
• “Website and photos are always extremely slow to load… please get this fixed and all is good.”• “Very buggy. Not intuitive at times… back feature does not work.” (Users often conflate bugs with slowness.)• “It runs slow, no reason to have hosting servers that run slowly in 2025.”HighMobile App Availability - 1.8%
• No app… overall just a brutal website.”• “An app.” (Single‑word but explicit request)• “Prefer to use Redfin because it has a better app.”MediumCollaboration & Sharing - 1.5%
• Bring back “Notes” (49)• Tagging or folders for Favorites (56)• Thumbnails included in share links (56)• We don’t like that we can’t take notes anymore on each house, bring back notes.”• “I need to be able to categorize favorites.” (Tags/folders request)• “When I share a link, there is no thumbnail, makes it less user friendly.”MediumAccess & Auth (Login/Account) - 1.2%
• “Asked for password reset and never sent to me… you have no customer support number either.”• “Make it easier to log in and stay logged in.”• “You won’t let people change their email or delete their accounts… shouldn’t have to contact a realtor to do that.”• “No app, always have to log back in… overall just a brutal website.” (Auth + mobile frustration bleed together.)
Notifications & Emails - 1%
• Sold alerts for saved homes• Price‑drop alerts• Stop auto‑shutoff of alerts• “Notify you when a home you liked or saved has been sold so you don’t go back trying to find it.”• “Alerts shut off due to customer inactivity — don’t shut me off if I didn’t tell you to shut off.”• “I’d like price‑drop alerts.”
Personalization & Relevance - 0.6%
• “Only send active listings.”• “Too many properties outside of search scope—a horrible waste of time.”• “Nothing is even close to what I want.”
Self‑Serve Search Controls - 0.1%
• “It is impossible to create brand‑new searches without contacting my realtor… I should be able to create and save new searches.”• “Make it easier to edit searches.”• “I’d like to adjust my preferences myself.”
Consumers consistently want:
• More control (filters + self‑serve search)• More complete data (utilities, taxes, flood‑zone, history)• Better visuals (floor plans, larger photos, 3D)• Less friction (navigation, login, sharing)• Stronger map tools## Survey Chatbot### (5.2%) apart from 12 product themes mentioned above.
• Complaints about survey timing/length, the AI/chatbot, and survey pop‑ups/welcome gates.
• “Ask this AFTER I have used it rather than when I am starting.”• “No survey When just starting.”• “Have this survey done after 3 or 5 times being logged in. You will get better information.”• “get rid of ChatBot!” (n=45)#### Consumer‑focused changes to implement:
• Delay the first survey until an engagement threshold (eg ≥ 3 logins OR ≥5 minutes total on site OR ≥2 listing detail views). This directly answers the “ask me later” feedback.• Frequency cap (1 survey prompt per 14 days) and grace period for new users, never prompt twice in the same session.• “Help us improve OneHome (1–2 minutes).” Make it clear the survey is optional and won’t block content.• Move the first ask to email (e.g., after a first‑week alert) or in‑product micro‑banner that doesn’t block content, instead of a pop‑up/welcome overlay. This addresses the “pop‑up/gate” frustration• Given the “remove AI bot” sentiment (45 mentions), default to inline feedback links (“Tell us what’s missing”) and simple thumbs‑up/down first; allow AI assistant as an opt‑in for users who want conversational help.• Keep the core survey to ≤ 5 questions; anything longer goes behind an explicit “Take full survey” link. “Too long” and “too early” are the most common timing critiques (23)# Personas:
Who are the key personas?
Primary Users
• Homebuyers and Renters: Individuals using OneHome to explore properties, neighborhoods, and market trends to make informed purchase or rental decisions.• Agents and Broker Offices: Real estate professionals leveraging OneHome to provide clients with comprehensive property, neighborhood, and market insights during the buying journey.Secondary Users
• MLS Members and Staff: Users accessing market and property intelligence through Matrix and MLS-Touch, benefiting from enhanced neighborhood and market data integrated into the OneHome ecosystem.# Critical to Quality:
• Feature Continuity: Ensure uninterrupted delivery of neighborhood insights (schools, lifestyle, commute, boundaries) using updated LiveBy Boundaries API.• Data Accuracy & Recency: All neighborhood, market, commute, and climate data must be authoritative, up-to-date, and clearly timestamped.• Geographic Precision: Insights must align accurately with neighborhood boundaries, parcels, and school attendance zones.• Rich Market Analytics: Market stats should include dynamic charts (price trends, AVM ranges, neighborhood averages) and interactive comparisons, not just static tax/sale history.• Optimized Information Hierarchy: Property detail pages must follow a clear, user-centric hierarchy.• Floor Plans Integration: Provide interactive floor plans, enable zoom and pan functionality for detailed viewing.• Modern, User-Friendly Presentation: Replace text-heavy layouts and outdated styles (e.g.: thin, light fonts and oversized graphics) with clean, balanced designs.• Performance: Data must load quickly without negatively impacting listing or map page performance.• Mobile Responsiveness: All insights should be optimized for mobile-first experiences with intuitive navigation and touch-friendly components.• Accessibility Compliance: Meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards (color contrast, keyboard navigation, alt text for charts, readable tooltips).• Immersive Visualization Performance: Street View and 3D Fly-Around must load quickly without degrading listing page performance.• Closing Cost Calculator: Provide accurate, region‑specific closing cost estimates (taxes, fees, prepaid items) with clear assumptions and transparent line items.• Mortgage Pre-qualifier: Deliver a fast, soft‑check affordability estimate. Must be simple, privacy‑first, clearly explained, and avoid implying loan approval while providing reliable purchase‑power guidance.• Pricing History: Provide complete, clearly formatted price history with accurate sale dates and listing events# Quant & Qual Analysis & Insights:
Quantitative Analysis
• Usage Tracking: Measure how often users interact with neighborhood insights modules, including market stats charts, demographics breakdown, POI chips, commute cards, school tabs, climate tiles. (e.g., clicks, hovers, time spent, shares).• Conversion Impact: Use A/B tests to compare listings with enhanced insights vs. baseline for actions like Save, Contact Agent, and Compare.• Heatmaps & Funnel Analysis: Visualize navigation patterns to understand how insights influence user flow and decision-making.Qualitative Analysis
• Surveys & Interviews: Assess how new insights affect buyer confidence and perceived value.• User Testing: Validate clarity, usability, and trustworthiness of enhanced modules (market stats, demographics, schools, lifestyle, commute, climate).• Agent Feedback: Gather input on whether insights improve advisory conversations and client engagement.# Business Case:
Providing deeper property, neighborhood, and market insights positions OneHome as a more valuable tool for homebuyers, renters, and agents. By delivering richer data for school, market, monthly payments, demographics, amenities with modern appealing UI experiences and Immersive visualization experiences, we can:
• Improve decision-making efficiency and shorten time-to-close, increasing overall platform usage and customer satisfaction.• Strengthen agent value by equipping professionals with comprehensive local market knowledge and interactive tools that enhance client trust.The real estate search experience is increasingly competitive, and homebuyers expect context, guidance, and personalization when evaluating homes. Current OneHome features (e.g.: basic tax/sales record) are helpful but insufficient. By embedding hyperlocal insights and advanced market analytics, OneHome can:
• Drive faster purchase decisions by reducing research friction and consolidating key data in one place.• Improve user retention and engagement through enriched, interactive experiences (like fly-around and interactive floor plans) and visually modern UI and user-centric information hierarchy update.• Provide agents with tools that reinforce their advisory role, making OneHome indispensable in client conversations.• Create new monetization opportunities through data licensing, local advertising, and premium insights offerings.# Success Metrics/KPIs/OKRs### Objective: Increase engagement with property and neighborhood insights to help users make more informed, confident decisions.
KPIs:
• Key Performance Indicators (KPIs):
• +15% increase in time spent per listing with insights modules.• +10% increase in Save or Contact Agent actions on listings with enhanced insights and Street View/Fly-Around..• −10% reduction in bounce rate on listing pages.• ≥30% of users interact with at least one neighborhood insights feature (market stats, schools, commute, POI, climate).• +10% increase in Request Tour after interacting with market or neighborhood insights.• +10% increase in Like/Dislike, Notes, Share, and Compare interactions.• −15% reduction in duplicate listing views using Seen indicators.• ≥80% favorable agent satisfaction score (via post-launch survey).• Example OKRs:
Objective: Improve the decision-making experience for homebuyers on OneHome.
• KR1: Launch the upgrade with regard to the OneHome Upkeep & Hyperlocal Neighbourhood Insights features (market stats, schools, lifestyle or POI, commute, climate, streetview) for top 25 MLS regions.• KR2: Achieve +25% increase in listing page engagement by Q1 2027.• KR3: Reach 90% satisfaction rate in post-visit surveys regarding data usefulness and clarity.# Changes needed:
• Removed all references to SDAP migration and fallback strategies (not applicable at this time). Maintain LiveBy as the primary source for neighborhood insights (LiveBy API (https://docs.liveby.com/))• Introduce rich market insights similar to competitors, including estimated property value ranges and neighborhood-level home value distributions.• Enrich demographics and school related information.• Add dog parks around neighborhoods• Add Floor Plans feature. (According to WAV Group Floor Plan Consumer Interest Survey (2025), 82% of consumers believe floor plans should be required on all listings)• Update information hierarchy for property detail, and Monthly Payment section, and consider dual-layer content strategy (like http://Homes.com (http://Homes.com) with a popped up window for detailed full MLS data)• Improve content strategy (information hierarchy) to ensure navigation is intuitive and visuals aesthetically pleasing - texts, size, white space, colour.• Improve UI/UX with visual cards with icons for property details, interactive maps for POIs and commute.• Replace outdated school and lifestyle modules with trendy UI patterns.• Add climate risk analytics and environmental factors (flood, wildfire, wind, etc.) with visual gauges or scenario projections or simply connects to 3rd party vender like First Street to obtain full climate risk reports.• Enhance property and neighborhood exploration by integrating Street-Level View, Shadowmap or Fly-Around capabilities.• Place language switcher UI/UX to the Profile with native names and instant apply• Add Listing Metrics (example, 20K views, favorited by 3K)• Add listing “seen” tags and option to add feedback• Add closing cost calculator and mortgage pre-qualifier for user affordability experience# High Level Requirement
• Display Neighborhood Insights
Present schools, lifestyle amenities, commute options, demographics, and climate risk alongside individual property details in a visually modern, interactive form• Market Stats & Trends
Move beyond basic tax/sale history to include:
• Median sale price and AVM ranges from multiple providers (e.g., Collateral Analytics, Quantarium).• Neighborhood-level comparisons (e.g., vs metro and national averages).• Interactive charts for price trends and market dynamics.• Enriched School Insights
• Attendance boundaries, grades, and school type.• Additional metrics (enrollment, student count, ratings).• Features: school photos, official website links, reviews, and contact details.• Expanded Demographic Information
Include household income, racial composition, community characteristics, and language distribution for deeper neighborhood context.• Lifestyle & Commute Insights
• POI chips for categories like Grocery, Parks, Dog Parks, Fitness, Nightlife.• Commute cards with isochrone overlays and multi-destination comparisons.• Climate Risk Insights:
• Display composite climate risk score with integrated third-party data (e.g., First Street®).• Include flood, fire, wind, air quality, and heat risk indicators.• Provide links and clear source attribution for transparency.• Immersive Visualization:
• Street View Integration:
• Embed Google Street View or equivalent for interactive street-level navigation.• Allow users to explore the property’s surroundings.• Fly-Around (3D Aerial View):
• Provide a dynamic 3D view of the property and neighborhood.• Features include zoom and rotate for a realistic exploration experience.• Highlight nearby POIs and transit options within the 3D environment.• Sunlight/Shadowmap (Future enhancement)
• Sunlight simulation for solar exposure analysis.• Engagement and Collaboration
• Show a “Seen” badge (and last‑viewed timestamp) on cards and detail pages so buyers can track progress during long sessions.• Surface Views and Favorites with time‑bounded context (e.g., “861 views · 16 favorites · in last 3 days”).• Group Like and Dislike with Notes and Share in a compact “My Activity” near the price/media so its visible and intuitive.• Provide Share (copy link / email) for fast collaboration with family; default to not sending engagement metrics to external recipients.• Add info icons + timestamps on metrics, user preference to hide popularity metrics.• Floor Plans Feature
• Add a dedicated Floor Plans tab or banner button on property detail pages.• Support multi-floor navigation (dropdown or tabs for 1st, 2nd, 3rd floor). (Ideally)• Enable zoom and pan functionality for detailed viewing. (Ideally)• Ability to “walk through” the home in 3D. (Ideally)• Ensure mobile responsiveness and fast loading.• Support specific room dimensions.• Clearly communicate how measurements were obtained• Use recognized measurement standards where possible (e.g., ANSI)• Include disclaimers but strive for consistency and confidence.• Consumers want more than a sketch — they want precision.
Most valued elements:
• Overall home layout• Room-by-room dimensions• Overall square footage of the home• Lot size / acreageLess detail = lower perceived value.• Floor plans should be displayed in the same place on every listing (4th Page)• Optimized Information Hierarchy
• Apply spec-first ordering: Basic Info → Amenities → Cost-critical info → Administrative → Structural.• Bundle cost-related information for quick benchmarking.• Implement dual-layer approach: Surface layer for quick lifestyle facts, deep layer for MLS-grade data.• Mortgage Pre-Qualifier
• Provides early affordability clarity for buyers, reducing uncertainty and price shock later in the journey.• Helps buyers self‑qualify faster, leading to more confident saves, inquiries, and offers.• Strengthens agent conversations by signaling buyer readiness without exposing sensitive financial data.• Reduces friction by keeping users in‑experience instead of redirecting to third‑party lender sites.• Positions OneHome as a decision‑support platform, not just a listing viewer.
• Risk of over‑reliance on estimates, which may differ from final loan terms and cause confusion if not clearly framed.Requirement
Phase
Note
Design Considerations
Solution ConsiderationsDisplay Neighborhood Insights
As a Home Buyer, I want to see neighborhood insights (schools, lifestyle, commute, demographics, climate risk) so that I understand the full context of a property
Visually modern, interactive, mobile‑first layout; grouped insight sections
Integrate APIs for schools, POI, demographics, commute, and climate datasetsMarket Stats & Trends
As a user, I want to see market stats & trends so that I can gauge pricing and make competitive decisions
Includes median price, AVMs, and neighborhood comparisons
Clear trend charts; intuitive benchmark visuals
Connect to multiple AVM providers; data normalization
Enriched School Insights
As a user, I want enriched school insights so that I can assess education options near a property
Map boundary overlays
Includes boundaries, grades, enrollment, ratings and periodic updates from providersAs a user, I want to view school photos, links, and reviews so that I can better evaluate schools
Media cards
school media from data partnersExpanded Demographic Information
As a user, I want demographic information so that I can understand the community profile
Includes income, race, language, household composition
Accessible charts; neutral presentation
demographic data pipelinesLifestyle & Commute Insights
As a user, I want lifestyle & POI insights so that I can see what amenities are nearby
POI chips (grocery, parks, pet friendly, fitness, nightlife)
multi-destination comparisons
Interactive filters; map overlays
POI extraction via map provider APIsAs a Commuter, I want commute insights so that I can compare travel times to multiple destinations
interactive toggle options
Climate Risk Insights
I want climate risk insights so that I understand environmental exposure
Risk icons; prominent source labeling
Climate dataset ingestion + score calculation\As a User, I want detailed climate indicators so that I can assess specific hazards
Layered risk icons; color coding
Combine multiple risk APIs and datasetsImmersive Visualization
I want Street View so that I can explore the neighborhood visually
Already have Pegman Drag - needs to be improved
Google Street View on instant clickI want a 3D aerial fly‑around so that I can see the property in context
Enhances spatial understanding
Smooth 3D navigation, POI markers
3D /imagery integrationI want future sunlight/shadow simulation so that I understand lighting conditions
Time‑slider UI
Sun simulation engine integrationEngagement and Collaboration
As a User, I want a “Seen” badge so that I know which listings I’ve already reviewed
Subtle markers/tags, timestamps
As a User, I want engagement metrics (views, favorites) so that I understand listing popularity
Compact placement over main area / image
As a User, I want all my activity in one place (likes, dislikes, notes, shares) so that I can easily manage preferences
Grouped action panel
As a User, I want simple sharing so that I can send listings to others quickly
Clean share UI
privacy rulesOptimized Information Hierarchy
As a User, I want information to be logically ordered so that it’s easier to digest
Mortgage Pre-Qualifier
As a Buyer, I want cost information bundled together so that I can evaluate affordability quickly
I want quick facts easily accessible with deeper MLS data when needed so that I can browse efficiently
I want a mortgage pre‑qualifier so that I understand affordability early
I want to stay within OneHome during financing steps so that I don’t lose context
I want clear disclaimers so that I’m not misled by estimates#### UI/UX Considerations
• Add a “Explore Neighborhood” button on listing pages with options for Street View and Fly-Around.• Ensure all visualizations (charts, maps, scorecards, 3D views) are easy to interpret, interactive, and mobile-friendly.• Use icons and cards for property details to improve scannability.Data Sources
• LiveBy remains primary for neighborhood insights with Updated API https://docs.liveby.com/guides/boundaries/getting-started (https://docs.liveby.com/guides/boundaries/getting-started)• MLS Data: For market stats, inventory insights, and agent/broker insights.• Google Maps API: For map rendering, geolocation, and POI visualization.Data Governance & Updates
• Ensure all data is sourced from authoritative and up-to-date databases.• Maintain clear source attribution and methodology links for transparency.### Questions / Assumptions / Open Issues
• Market Data Scope
• Which specific market metrics will deliver the most value to users and agents?
(e.g., AVM ranges, price per square foot, inventory trends, rental vs. ownership mix)• Cost & Licensing Considerations
• What are the limitations or costs associated with accessing third-party market data sources (e.g., AVM providers)• Street View & Fly-Around:
• What are the API usage costs for Google Maps Street View and 3D Fly-Around?• Are there any restrictions on embedding these features within OneHome’s property detail pages?• Should Fly-Around include POI overlays and sun simulation as part of the initial release or future roadmap?• Data Quality Handling
• How will we handle discrepancies in data or outdated information?• Agent Customization
• Will agents have the ability to customize or prioritize certain insights (e.g., highlight schools, hide climate risk, emphasize market stats)?• Climate Risk Visibility
• Determine whether to display climate risk data for all properties or implement a strategy to control visibility (e.g., show in a secondary tab or behind an info icon).• Rationale: Direct exposure of climate risk scores may negatively impact perceived attractiveness of certain listings and create tension with agents and sellers who prioritize marketing appeal.• Decision required: Define UX approach that balances transparency with business sensitivity.# Competitor Analysis
For detailed competitor analysis, please see: Competitor Analysis: Listing Detail Page Hierarchy & Feature Strategy - Google Sheets (https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1bciUbYVfN_sfB5zp8N6JNNQCIwclJ4wXMl1fOmX0u9Y/edit?gid=0#gid=0)
Below are ideal features observed in competitors
1. Market insights from homes.com
Estimated Property Value (Multi-AVM Comparison)
• Displays a range of estimated property values• Includes multiple AVM providers for transparency and credibility• Shows average value and list price for context.• Provides interactive historical trend charts (1-Year and 5-Year views) for property value changes over time.Home Values in the Area & Comparative Analytics
• Displays a distribution chart of home values in the area, highlighting where the current property falls• Offers a toggle between Values and Values Per Sq Ft, giving users flexibility in how they interpret• Shows average home value comparisons among Neighborhood, Metro Area, and U.S. Average• UI: Uses clear visual hierarchy with bar charts and line graphs for easy interpretation• School Information – http://Realtor.com (http://Realtor.com) Example (Strong UI & Data Presentation)• Clear Section Header: “Schools” with an info icon for quick clarification.• School List with Key Details:
• Each school entry includes:
• Name (e.g., Ps 59 Beekman Hill International).• Type & Grades (Public, K–5).• Student Enrollment Count (e.g., 523 students).• Distance from Property (e.g., 0.2 mi).• District Information (New York City Geographic District #2).• Parent Rating displayed as star icons for quick visual assessment.• School Score Badge: Prominent numeric rating (e.g., 8 or 9) in a colored circle for easy scanning.• Actionable CTA: “See all schools on map” button for interactive exploration.• Source Attribution: Location data from Precisely; ratings from GreatSchools for credibility.• Transit & Commute Factors – http://Homes.com (http://Homes.com)Clear Section Headers:
• Transit and Area Factors separated for easy scanning.• Transit Details:
• Subway Stations: Name (e.g., Kosciuszko Street), line badges (J, M, Z), and walk time (e.g., 12 min).• Bus Stops: Name and walk time (e.g., 2 min).• Airports: Name and drive time (e.g., LaGuardia – 20 min).Area Factors with Scores:
• Crime Score: Numeric (e.g., 3/10) with label “Low Crime.”• Walk Score: Numeric (e.g., 85/100) with label “Very Walkable.”• Bike Score: Numeric (e.g., 74/100) with label “Very Bikeable.”• Transit Score: Numeric (e.g., 93/100) with label “Rider’s Paradise.”• Demographic Information – HouseSigma Example (Comprehensive Data & Visual Presentation)Clear Section Header:
• Demographic stats grouped under a clean header with source attribution (Statistics Canada – 2021 Census).Key Metrics Displayed:
• Population• Average Household Income• Average Age• Household Size• Immigrants• Renters• Education: College/University• Average Home Value• Low Income:• Not in Labour Force• Single• Households with ChildrenInteractive Tabs for Deep Dive:
• Categories include Household Income, Age, Education, Ethnicity (Top 10), Language (Top 10), Religion, Construction, Occupation, Housing, Commute Method.Visual Representation:
• Pie Chart for Household Income distribution with color-coded ranges (e.g., $0–$29,999, $30,000–$59,999, $60,000–$79,999, etc.).Source Attribution:
• Data sourced from Statistics Canada – 2021 Census for credibility.5. Climate Risks & Sun Exposure from redfin.
Clear Section Headers:
• Climate Risks and Sun Exposure separated for clarity.Climate Risk Factors:
• Each risk displayed with icon, label, and severity level (e.g., Flood Factor – Minimal, Heat Factor – Severe).• Additional context in smaller font:
• Flood: “Unlikely to flood in next 30 years.”• Heat: “7 days above 99° expected this year, 15 days in 30 years.”• Wind: “39% chance of strong winds in next 30 years.”• Air: “6 unhealthy days expected this year.”• CTA: “View full report” for detailed analysis.• Source Attribution: Data provided by First Street for credibility.• Full Report (First Street):
• Interactive map with property pin and risk scores (e.g., Flood 1/10, Fire 1/10, Wind 6/10, Air 5/10, Heat 7/10).• Key insights: nearby pollution sources, tornado/wind history, air quality ranking.• Transparent methodology and peer-reviewed claim for trust.• Sun Exposure:
• Displays average sunlight hours (e.g., 8.7 hrs in June, 4.4 hrs in December).• Comparison to U.S. average for context.• CTA: “View sun simulation” for interactive experience.• Source Attribution: Data provided by Shadowmap.Sun Simulation (Shadowmap):
• 3D visualization of property and surrounding buildings.• Dynamic sun path simulation with time-of-day slider for precise solar exposure analysis.• Actionable design: CTAs and tab navigation (location, flood, fire, heat, wind, air, sun simulation) enable users to explore multiple risk maps and simulations seamlessly.6. Neighborhood & POI from Realtor.com
Clear Section Header:
• Neighborhood & Schools with map visualization at the top for immediate context.Interactive Map:
• Displays clustered POI icons (restaurants, cafes, parks, shopping, etc.) on a detailed map.• Layer toggle for switching between categories (Nearby, Lifestyle, Transportation).• Map powered by Yelp for POI data.• Interactive Map with Category Filters:
• When Nearby is selected, a filter panel appears on the map allowing users to toggle POI categories:
• Food & Drink• Groceries• Shopping• Health & Safety• Community• Active & Outdoor• Arts & Entertainment• Dog Parks• Each category dynamically updates the map with relevant POI icons.POI Categories Listed Below Map:
• Parks• Shopping• Nightlife• Cafes• Groceries• Restaurants• DaycaresDistance Indicators:
• Each POI example includes distance from property (e.g., 0.1 mi).Source Attribution:
• Scores provided by Local Logic; Locations provided by Yelp for credibility.• Fly-Around from Realtor.com#### Feature Overview
• http://Realtor.com (http://Realtor.com) integrates a “Fly Around” option within the property detail page.• This feature uses Google Maps 3D aerial view to allow users to explore the property and surrounding neighborhood from multiple angles.#### UI/UX Implementation
• Placement:
• The “Fly Around” button is located in the photo carousel alongside other media options (Exterior, Kitchen, Floor Plan).• This makes the feature discoverable without cluttering the page.• Interaction:
• Clicking “Fly Around” opens a full-screen interactive map with 3D buildings and POI markers.• Users can zoom and rotate for a realistic aerial exploration.• Visual Context:
• POIs such as restaurants, parks, dog parks and businesses are displayed directly on the map.• Floor Plan from RedfinPlacement:
• The “Floor Plans” button is prominently displayed on the property overview page, alongside other media options such as Photos and Street View.Interaction:
• Clicking “Floor Plans” opens a full-screen viewer with a dark background for contrast.• Users can toggle between floors using a dropdown menu (e.g., 1st floor, 2nd floor, 3rd floor).• The floor plan image is zoomable and pannable, allowing detailed inspection of rooms and layout.Visual Context:
• Each floor plan clearly labels rooms (e.g., Kitchen, Living Room, Deck).• Navigation controls are simple and intuitive, with the floor selector positioned at the bottom-left corner.• Attribution and disclaimers (e.g., “Results Reliable But Not Guaranteed”) are displayed for transparency.• Pricing History:• Provide a complete event‑based price timeline
Include all key events (Listed, Price Changes, Contingent/Pending, Sold, Relisted/Withdrawn) similar to Zillow, Redfin, and Realtor.• Show clear % increases/decreases alongside the price, with color coding (green ↑, red ↓) for instant scannability.• Provide context for value changes over time, matching competitors (Zillow, Redfin, Homes.com (http://Homes.com)).• Present them together under a single “Property History” module, following http://Realtor.com (http://Realtor.com) ’s cohesive design• Use expandable “Show more” behavior• Improve UI clarity and hierarchy - Use modern typography, spacing, and visual grouping to make historical data easier to read and interpret.• Add clear source attribution + timestamp• Realtor.com+ is positioned as a collaborative portal designed to keep agents and MLSs at the center of the home‑search experience. It provides shared environments where agents and consumers can jointly search, message, plan tours, and review insights.• Shared searches & listings inside a protected, branded space.• Real-time visibility into client activity: searches, saves, shares.• In‑platform messaging to reduce email/text fragmentation.• Integration of MLS-based filters and local market intel.• Leaning into AI driven tools. OneHome could differentiate with the following:
• Personalized recommendations (Feels tailor-made, easier discovery, faster progression to decision):
• AI‑curated property feeds (like Netflix-style home discovery)• recommendations: “Because you liked X and saved Y, we found.. ”• Neighborhood and lifestyle matching, not just property characteristics• Automated insights
• Provide quick market summaries for their chosen neighborhoods• Surface important updates automatically:
• Price drops• New schools info• Community trends• Deliver simple, human-readable insights like: “Homes in this area are selling 20% faster this month.”• Predictive engagement signals
• Notify customers when they’re likely to miss out: “This home will probably get an offer soon.”• Remind them gently if a saved home is time-sensitive: “You saved this 3 days ago — want to schedule a tour?”• Detect shifting preferences and auto-adjust recommendations.• AI Messaging• AI-enhanced tour planning or property evaluation tools• Realtor.com+ emphasizes mobile-first design• Evaluate the following:
• Speed of interactions• In-app messaging• Auto-generated route planning (Realtor.com+ explicitly calls this out)• Information Hierarchy Research: Please see the research on the excel document:Top OneHome Client Portal Data to Consider:
• Neighbourhood insights/Demographics
• Home price range• Population• Household average age• Household average income• Renters %• Education levels• Average home value• Language• Commute method• Unemployment• Marital Status• Parks• Average House size range• Transit options• Environmental Factors• Scores
• Walk/bike/transit• Environmental Factors
• Noise• Flood/heat/wind/fire/air• Parks/Green spaces• Nightlife• Shoping• Fitness• Pet friendly## Neighborhood-Level Market Data
• Median Sale Price by Home Type – SFH, townhouse, condo, duplex.• Home Turnover Rate – How often do people sell and move?• Rental Market Trends – Short-term vs. long-term rental demand.• Foreclosure & Distressed Property Rates – Investor potential vs. risk.• New Construction vs. Resale Market Split – Impact on pricing.• Zoning & Land Use – Impact on ADUs, multi-family units, commercial conversions.• Investor Activity – Percentage of homes bought by investors.## Environmental & Climate Risks
• Flood Zones & FEMA Mapping – Required flood insurance areas.• Earthquake Risk Zones – Structural resilience concerns.• Air Quality Index – Pollution impact on health.• Water Quality Reports – Drinking water safety.• Severe Weather History – Hurricanes, tornadoes, extreme temperatures.• Wildfire Risk – Proximity to high-risk areas.

pages.title: Portal Ads + Audience Intelligence  - (Cordless)
pages.content:
# Overview:
Portal ads are advertising placements designed to monetize traffic while enhancing relevance for users. They integrate directly into the user journey rather than appearing as external pop‑ups or third‑party banners while Audience Intelligence is a capability designed to provide deep insights into user segments, behaviors, and preferences by aggregating and analyzing data across multiple touchpoints. Portal Ads will utilize the audience intelligence to enable targeted advertising within OneHome to generate revenue and improve user experience .
The combination of these features will enable OneHome to understand who our audiences are, how they interact with the product, and what drives engagement or conversion. In this implementation with cordless technology, the intelligence gathered will be anonymous which protects the privacy of our users and will simply categorize users based on their habits, using that information to surface relevant campaigns to them.
https://ads.cordlessmedia.com/documentation/index.html#display.md (https://ads.cordlessmedia.com/documentation/index.html#display.md)# Customer Segment (VOM, VOC,VOBA):
VOC# Personas:
The Home buyer (User) persona; A property seeker who values relevance and transparency. Designing for this persona ensures ads enhances their journey rather than detract from it, balancing monetization with user trust.#
Objective:
• Enable targeted advertising: Provide advertisers with the ability to reach specific user segments effectively.• Generate new revenue streams: Monetize portal traffic through ad placements and campaigns.• Offer flexible ad formats: Support banners and native ads.• Personalized recommendations based on preferences (budget, location, lifestyle).• Behavioral insights Track browsing habits, content preferences, and engagement frequency.Risk: User resistance to ads <> Mitigation: Use non-intrusive formats and frequency capping.
Risk: Data privacy concerns <> Mitigation: Transparent policies and opt-in mechanisms. Anonymous data.# Critical to Quality:
Visio link (https://corelogic-my.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/personal/sobasi_cotality_com/_layouts/15/doc2.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B69FD1A63-7315-4A25-9657-4C1168E72A8D%7D&file=Drawing%204.vsdx&action=edit&mobileredirect=true&wdnewandopenct=0&ct=1765245368852&wdOrigin=OFFICECOM-WEB.START.NEW&wdPreviousSessionSrc=HarmonyWeb&wdPreviousSession=6eb0af77-b350-4b4d-b5bf-124d86520b2e&cid=8374a15f-36b0-4c54-a12e-aea9fecac1f9&or=PrevCreateNew)# Quant & Qual Analysis & Insights:
What is our data telling us? Quantitatively and QualitativelyWhat is the outcome of our competitor analysis?
Realtor.com (http://Realtor.com)# Business Case:
Potential Revenue Generation
Assets:### Include any needed early sketches, presentations, screenshots from competitors, etc. for context# Proposition:
2 options
• Banner• Native AdsLocation : Property details page#
Success Metrics/KPIs/OKRs### *What are the success metrics that indicate you’re achieving your goal?
*2023 OneHome Success Metrics Pillars
Consumer Engagement & Adoption
• Increase in revenue from Ads• Enhanced user engagement through relevant ad targeting.• Positive feedback from advertisers and portal users.#
High-Level Requirements### Requirements
Used to capture initial business requirements for design & solutioning
Team

Design Considerations
Solution ConsiderationsAs a user, I want to see ads in clearly defined slots so that they don’t disrupt my browsing experience.
As a user, I want ads to be contextually relevant to my search so that they feel useful rather than intrusive.
As a user, I want frequency capping so that I don’t see the same ad too often.
As a user, I want privacy compliance so that my data is handled responsibly.
Consent Manager
As a system I will have this feature configurable per MLS
As a system, i will have Configuration to control Banner vs Native ads independently per MLS
This is to hedge against any pushback received from MLSs### Questions / Assumptions / Open Issues
