pages.title: CoreLogic MLS Data
pages.content:
See also .Product Manager and SME: Ryan PersichilliFulfillment Team: CincinnatiIs this data in OneSource: NoIs this data in the OneSouce queue: YesProposed internal data acquisition process:• A subset, or living data sample, will be available in OneSource• This subset of production data will be used for POC's, data exploration and cursory models• Once use cases are defined, a formal request for a complete dataset must be submitted, processed and signed off• Based on the permissible use restrictions, any dataset provided by the Cincinnati team is allowed one purpose only and may not be passed around• If the data are used in a model, there are preset royaltiesPermitted Use of MLS Data Solutions (Internal Info Only):Use of MLS Data is limited to Risk Management and Valuation purposes. Risk Management uses include activities related to:• Identifying, assessing, evaluating and/ or monitoring risks associated with real property transactions• Fraud detection and prevention• Underwriting• Credit and/or prepayment risk management• Asset management• Portfolio review• Quality control• Due diligence• Servicing surveillance• Property valuation estimation and verification.Non-Permitted Use of MLS Data Solutions:Non-permitted uses MLS data include• Redistribution of MLS Data or CL Derivatives in consumer-direct applications that permit consumers to use of data for personal, non-business purposes,• Redistribution for purposes other than Risk Management, including direct marketing programs that solicit consumers and MLS Members for goods and services,• Providing the MLS Data or CL Derivatives to clients in an unrestricted mannerDifferences in raw data sources:MLS Data SourcePermissible UseKey FactorsQuality GradePartner InfoNet (PIN)Risk Management and Valuation purposes only. Marketing use is strictly prohibited.Direct from MLSAll market listingsAll StatusesAll incremental changesNear real time updatesARealist Value Map (RVM)Risk Management and Valuation purposes only. Marketing use is strictly prohibited.May only be used within analytics or as a data derivative (e.g. AVMs, Models, and derived statistics) Any other use is prohibitedDirect from MLSAll market listingsAll StatusesAll incremental changesNear real time updatesAMLS5 (Syndicated)Risk Management and Valuation purposes only• Active listings only from 3rd party syndicator ListHub• Subset of a market’s total active listingsNo Close Date/StatusNo DOM*• ListHub data includes for sale and for rent listings. | B |MLS4 (Aggregated)Risk Management and Valuation purposes only• Actives Only from 3rd party aggregator• Subset of a market’s total active listingsNo Close Date/StatusNo DOM*Missing data**• Altos has a separate rental listing dataset that we do not obtain. | C |FSBOCan be used in both Risk Management Valuation, and for marketing purposes• Listings scraped from http://www.forsalebyowner.com/ (http://www.forsalebyowner.com/).• Actives Only from direct FSBO feed• Subset of a market’s active listingsNo Close Date/No StatusNo DOM* | B - C |All Revenue generating uses of MLS data regardless of source require an allocation for royalties. AVMs are the only exception to this rule.SourceCodenameDate RangeCoverageUsage RestrictionsCoreMLSDatabase like RealCore. Best for transactional access, not big extracts. Contains PIN, MLS4, MLS5, FSBO, and ValueMap data.AltosMLS4FSBOListHubMLS5Provides about 400-500K net additional listings over PIN.We pay a fixed royalty (no additional fees required for additional use) and are allowed to display these listings.Partner InfoNetPoint2 (http://www.point2.com/)Good – similar to ListHub.Used in Chase's MyNewHome app. No rights to use elsewhere in CoreLogic.Realist ValueMap/K2# NotesWe have been asked to refer to the third party sources that have codenames above by their anonymous titles (MLS4, MLS5, etc) in routine communication where possible and never name them in external communication such as with clients.The 'MLS Data Permitted Use Sales Guide 07-25-2012' is available for internal use only upon request.# Master Data DictionaryFrom Partner InfoNet MLS Before providing data to clients or selling a product (e.g. AVM) that uses the data in any way, it is necessary to set up appropriate revenue sharing with the MLS boards via Ryan Persichilli.## GreenBox DB
TYPE: SQLServerSERVER NAME: new as of 2013-12-05 = DOMLSNA01SAGG01 (10.183.101.33), old = FACLSNA01SSQL12 (10.183.100.74)PORT: 1433DB: User_MACRAUsers: Carrie Bennett, David Lisuk, Dingxi Qiu, Harold Dickerson, Jason Hu, Jianjun Xie, John Jensen, Liang Tian, Matt Cannon, Matthias Blume, Tricia Murray, Xiaolin Tan, Scott LeishmanThe database contains one table for Partner InfoNet (MACRA_PIN) and a separate table for Realist ValueMap/K2 boards (MACRA_TRENDS). The licensing agreements with the MLS boards permit us to show values from Partner InfoNet to clients. They prohibit us from showing any values from Realist ValueMap to clients – only analytics/derived data that does not enable reverse engineering the original MLS-provided values may be provided.Furthermore, we are not permitted to use the AgentRemarks ("private remarks") field except for analysis, even from the Partner InfoNet boards – not permitted to share this field's values with clients.The database contains all PIN and K2 MLS records that we receive from the MLS boards from 2006 (was 10/1/2011) to present, not just the most recent status.
